input:
Sarai Diaz

1. Challenge

We received a Housing Project in which the local citizens of a community were opposed  because they think it will affect the environment thanks to the corrupted information some  people has spread. The challenge the company presented us was to change the perception  of the Project in that community to have their support when the construction finally started.

2. Selection

I selected visualization because in order to change their perception first I need to  understand the real needs and concerns of the people and identify the messages they have  received from the opposing groups that are currently affecting the image of the Project.

3. Application

First, I needed to enlist all the stakeholders involved because it was not only the immediate  community but while digging I found out there also there were political interest, local  government, schools, and all kind of organizations that needed to be included in the  campaign. Then I needed to know what the people thought about the project in a deeply  perspective, because I couldn’t change what I didn’t know they think. That took me to small  interviews with key actor of the community and the identified groups to better understand  what we were dealing with, to let them express themselves, in these interviews we used  pictures of the project that lead us to emotions, when we showed images of the project  people were: angry, frustrated, sad and some of them tired of hearing about the project.

4. Insight

After that process I realized that the real mission was not to change their perception of the  Project but it was to really inform them of what was going to happen without the alarm,  misconceptions and mistakes the other group had already told them, people were angry,  sad, frustrated about the project. Also, being able to translate “the bad image of the  project” into something more specific like emotions, let me realize we needed an innovative  approach to capture their attention to be able to provide them the information.

5. Approach

Visualization is key before starting any activity to really understand what you are dealing  with and finding the best strategies to tackle it, but the problem is it takes time and your  boss, your client, or the one paying you will ask for immediate actions and immediate  results. So one of the most important things I would do next when I need to use visualization  is first sensibilize of the importance of visualization as a tool, not to guarantee success, but  to understand the challenge, your audience and guide you to a better solution.

input: Ivy is a type of climbing plant that is often found adorning the walls of buildings and gardens. Known for its lush, green leaves, ivy can provide a beautiful and natural covering for structures, adding both aesthetic appeal and environmental benefits. It is important to manage its growth, however, as ivy can sometimes become invasive, potentially damaging the surfaces it clings to.

input:
DESIGN THINKING ACTIVITY - 4

Project: Electric bikes

Team Number:  05

Team members: Karan Patel

TASK 1 - STORY TELLING IN PROBLEM FRAMING

Given below are some of the problems expressed by the

consumers

1. E-bikes tend to have low resale value.

2. Maintenance and repairs are too costly.

3. Riding range remains low.

4. Battery charge time is too long

5. Battery has a rather short life span

TASK 2 - STORY TELLING IN SOLUTION FRAMING

Technical Details:

Security:

 Air Bags for saving the life.

 Can be connected to smartphones.

 Tube less tires to avoid punchers in between of a journey.

 Adjustable height according to their convenience.

 It has gears as normal bikes with different power levels.

Tracking:

 It can be tracking through mobile in a smartphone manner

 So that we can check the status of the bike charging,

condition etc.

Battery Life:

 It has a good battery life that lasts more than the existing

bikes which makes you travel more distance.

 Capacity of the battery is 65V 30Ah

Performance:

 The acceleration rate is 20kmph in a second

 The brakes are disc brakes that acts instantly.

 The charging time is 5 hrs

 The battery lasts for 70 kms

Solutions given by designers to the

consumer problems:

1. Running Costs: As they are expensive as compared to

petrol bikes but when we think about the fuel and

electricity it will say that it wasn’t more expensive.

2. Motor Life: It can be used more than 8 years with running

25000kms without any single issue.

3. Resale Value: The resale value of E-Bike is less compared

to fuel bike but it is more friendly to the current generation

of people.

4. Battery Life: The battery life is good but you have to be

aware of some safety measures of the parts in E-Bike like

not making the bike over charging.

Advantages of E-Bike both Technically

and Financially:

 It is eco friendly that helps us to save the environment that

the present generation is wanted to do.

 It saves the wastage of money compared to fuel bikes as

the price of fuel is more now a days  Consumer Feedback:

 The seating is comfortable and easy to balancing. The height

problem is resolved for the short raiders.

 The E-Bike’s tracking system and airbag system helps to

monitor our vehicle and safety of the raider.

 The vehicle is smart and smooth to handle. Its battery is also

last good enough to a good ride. It is affordable to buy.

 I am glad to have product like this.

input:
Example of Reflection - Value Chain Analysis – Jhonatan De Oliveira

Chalenge & Selection

I, as a business consultant, usually use “value chain analysis” to better understand the  routine of the organization I am studying, so I can identify what inputs are needed to  perform a given activity, what value is generated in that activity, and what the outputs  generated.

The reality of each organization is different, so it is important to carefully study each of  the realities and try to identify inputs to be able to improve the day of these companies  and consequently improve the impact on their customers.

Application

Recently I had the opportunity to apply Value Chain Analysis tool to a public health  organization in the city of Rio de Janeiro / Brazil. The main challenge was to understand  how the logistics process of the health supply chain works. In this way, the challenge  began with the purchase of medicines and ended with the delivery of these drugs in  specialized pharmacies.

During the study of the process it was possible to validate previously raised assumptions.  One of the biggest problems of the whole process was the delay and bureaucracy in the  purchasing process, more specifically in the bidding process. A bidding process could last  up to 360 days, which impacted directly on all pharmacies and consequently end users.

The process had the following major steps: ordering, purchasing, storage and  distribution.

Insight & Approach

With the in-depth analysis of the “value chain analysis” tool I was able to learn that  customer's "travel mapping" view is just as important when understanding only the  process of running the organization. To generate a better solution is necessary for the  customer, and you can do it.

The course of design that seems to me to show that it is necessary to see things as non- linear, always try to awaken creativity and listen as people. We cannot use data to  propose solutions to realities that are not known. You are looking for a product or  customer service.

input:
Challenge and Selection

A key area of my job scope is to develop step-by-step digitalization guides for the  small and medium enterprises (SMEs) of various industries and sectors. The SMEs  are important to Singapore, collectively employing 70% of Singapore’s workforce and  account for almost half of Singapore’s GDP. Hence, digitalisation of SMEs can have  a profound impact on Singapore’s economy.

However, SMEs generally lack the technical know-how and find it difficult to divert  resources to digital transformation. Hence, it is important to develop a guide to make  going digital simple for the SMEs.

To develop a guide that is most suitable to the needs of the SMEs, I plan to conduct  a series of workshops with various SMEs, and use the Mind Mapping tool to  generate insights, collect information and create design criteria.

Application

In the first workshop that I conduct, I will gather information from the SMEs to  understand their experiences in digitalization. I will get each SME to fill up a form to  provide me with information regarding their digital transformation journey, such as:

-  Current mode business operations (manual vs digital)

-  Areas of improvement for business operations

-  Challenges that SMEs when digitalizing

-  Obstacles that are stopping them from digitalizing

In the second workshop, with the information consolidated, I will set up a room with  walls filled with the common problems faced by the SMEs in the industry and the  SMEs can go around the room to understand the problems and think about how  digitalization can come in to help solve the problems.

In the third workshop, SMEs will be put together in groups to share their experiences  and problems, and most importantly, how digitalization can help. The SMEs will then  work together, to discuss on how they want to digitally transform their businesses in  step-by-step manner.

Insight and Approach

We have vast amount of information and data regarding the SMEs, and we thought  we knew what SMEs need. So, we developed the guides for the SMEs without  gathering and consolidating in a structured manner. However, we noticed that SMEs  did not find the guides useful.

With the mind mapping tool, I believe that we can better organize the information  provided by the SMEs, draw insights from their businesses and scope the design

criteria to help generate better suited ideas to be included in the guides. Bringing the  SMEs together and getting them to map their needs and requirements together, will  provide SMEs with best-suited guidance in their digital journey.

It is important to view from the consumer perspective. In my case, SMEs are my  consumer. To provide them with a product, the step-by-step guide, that can benefit  them and help them digitalize, it is vital to hear from them on what they need and  want.

Written by: Md Hamid

input: In the world of music, a melody is a sequence of notes that are perceived as a single entity. It is the part of a song that is most memorable and often carries the emotional weight of the piece. Melodies can be simple or complex, and they are crafted using a variety of musical elements such as rhythm, pitch, and harmony. Understanding how to create a compelling melody is a fundamental skill for any composer or songwriter.
target: In the world of music, a melody is a sequence of notes that are perceived as a single entity. It is the part of a song that is most memorable and often carries the emotional weight of the piece. Melodies can be simple or complex, and they are crafted using a variety of musical elements such as rhythm, pitch, and harmony. Understanding how to create a compelling melody is a fundamental skill for any composer or songwriter.

input: The contributions of Faraday to the field of electromagnetism are monumental. Faraday, a self-taught scientist, discovered electromagnetic induction, which is the principle behind the electric transformer and generator. His experiments and findings laid the groundwork for the practical use of electricity, transforming it from a scientific curiosity into a powerful tool for technology and industry. Despite having little formal education, Faraday's intuitive understanding of the natural world and his innovative experimental techniques have left a lasting legacy in the scientific community.

His experiments and findings laid the groundwork for the practical use of electricity, transforming it from a scientific curiosity into a powerful tool for technology and industry. Despite having little formal education, ⟨NAME⟩Faraday⟨/NAME⟩'s intuitive understanding of the natural world and his innovative experimental techniques have left a lasting legacy in the scientific community.

input: In today's lesson, we'll explore the life of Jay, a renowned scientist known for his groundbreaking research in renewable energy. Jay began his career with a passion for environmental science, which led him to pursue a degree in chemical engineering. His innovative approach to solar panel technology has significantly increased efficiency, making renewable energy more accessible worldwide. Jay's work not only contributes to scientific advancement but also inspires future generations to pursue careers in sustainable development.

input: The Faraday is a unit of electric charge, named in honor of the contributions to the field of electromagnetism. It is equivalent to the charge of one mole of electrons, approximately 96,485 coulombs. This unit is crucial in electrochemistry, where it helps in calculating the amount of substance that will be deposited or dissolved during an electrochemical reaction. Understanding the Faraday is essential for students studying chemistry and physics, as it provides a bridge between theoretical concepts and practical applications in the laboratory.
target: The Faraday is a unit of electric charge, named in honor of the contributions to the field of electromagnetism. It is equivalent to the charge of one mole of electrons, approximately 96,485 coulombs. This unit is crucial in electrochemistry, where it helps in calculating the amount of substance that will be deposited or dissolved during an electrochemical reaction. Understanding the Faraday is essential for students studying chemistry and physics, as it provides a bridge between theoretical concepts and practical applications in the laboratory.

input:
Rosa Jaque

The tool of Visualization

Challenge: I am an architecture student, since the first day of my career I have been facing different

challenges. However, I can say that creativeness and discipline have helped me to overcome them. For this

assignment I chose a challenge that consists on designing a rural community school located in Mexico.  The

project must be functional, sustainable and must minimize costs; and I will solve it by using “The tool of

Visualization” or “Visual Thinking” so have broad options.

Selection: “Visual thinking” is a way to solve a problem from the creative side of the brain. It is also a way of

communication. Visualizing is important in the designing process, that’s why I chose this tool because as in

architecture it lets you think in a none-verbal way.

Application: Visual thinking does not mean just drawing but to go further than words. For this project I

would try to first visualize spaces, materials and most importantly, how the experience in that place I´m

imagining will be. To make it effectively, I´ll create images such as renders so I can show to other people my

ideas and explain them easily. My intention is to sell ideas, to convince people to buy a project even when

they haven’t even touched it.

On the other hand, most people think that using cheap materials or cheaper construction methods is not

particularly good looking or secure, but I would like to demonstrate that it is not true and the best way is

through images.

Insight: In my opinion, to innovate results from exploring different solutions or alternatives for particular

situations and I think visual thinking is perfect for that. By exercising the creative side of our minds we could

redesign solutions when we need it.

Approach: I will not apply a different tool but I’ll rather complement the process previously described with  photography, so I can get more ideas that can help me build bridges to bigger projects than I could imagine.
target:
⟨NAME⟩Rosa Jaque⟨/NAME⟩

input: In the world of physics, understanding the concept of miles is crucial for measuring distances. A mile is a unit of length commonly used in the United States to quantify the distance between two points. It is equivalent to 5,280 feet or approximately 1.609 kilometers. This measurement is essential in various fields, including transportation, where it helps in calculating travel distances and speeds. For instance, when planning a road trip, knowing the number of miles between destinations can aid in estimating travel time and fuel consumption.
target: In the world of physics, understanding the concept of miles is crucial for measuring distances. A mile is a unit of length commonly used in the United States to quantify the distance between two points. It is equivalent to 5,280 feet or approximately 1.609 kilometers. This measurement is essential in various fields, including transportation, where it helps in calculating travel distances and speeds. For instance, when planning a road trip, knowing the number of miles between destinations can aid in estimating travel time and fuel consumption.

input: The city of Salem, located in the state of Massachusetts, is renowned for its rich history and cultural significance. Known primarily for the infamous Salem witch trials of 1692, this city has become a symbol of the dangers of mass hysteria and injustice. Visitors to Salem can explore numerous historical sites, including the Salem Witch Museum and the House of the Seven Gables. The city's maritime history is also celebrated, with the Salem Maritime National Historic Site offering insights into its past as a bustling seaport. Today, Salem is a vibrant community that blends its historical roots with modern attractions, making it a fascinating destination for tourists and history enthusiasts alike.
target: The city of Salem, located in the state of Massachusetts, is renowned for its rich history and cultural significance. Known primarily for the infamous Salem witch trials of 1692, this city has become a symbol of the dangers of mass hysteria and injustice. Visitors to Salem can explore numerous historical sites, including the Salem Witch Museum and the House of the Seven Gables. The city's maritime history is also celebrated, with the Salem Maritime National Historic Site offering insights into its past as a bustling seaport. Today, Salem is a vibrant community that blends its historical roots with modern attractions, making it a fascinating destination for tourists and history enthusiasts alike.

input:
Cynthia Chatel

Example Reflection – Visualization

Challenge and Selection

Visualization is used in my daily work life to communicate and organize thought  in different ways. Using visual thinking enhances the way we see things. Since  working at a publishing company, I use this approach with workers and clients.

During a project at work a client wanted to rearrange his entire branding  strategy one week before his launch, a project we were working on for almost  five weeks. We delivered the first draft of the project which he approved and  then a few weeks after he was unhappy, so we were tasked in redoing the  entire project over in less than a week.

Application

I began the entire process by first doodling his new changes, then we  compared it with other successful designs from this and other clients, comparing  feedback and feedforwards. Then we put all our ideas together on the drawing  board.

With this strategy the client was ultimately thrilled with the new plan, including  the new ideas that were included.

Insight and Approach

I believe we became overconfident with our first ideas on the project at the  time, and that I did not check in with the client until almost the last week. I have  now learned to always check in with clients on a weekly basis.
target:
⟨NAME⟩Cynthia Chatel⟨/NAME⟩

Insight and Approach

I believe we became overconfident with our first ideas on the project at the  time, and that I did not check in with the client until almost the last week. I have  now learned to always check in with clients on a weekly basis.

input: The Curie temperature is a critical point where certain materials lose their permanent magnetic properties, which can be restored when the temperature is lowered. This concept is essential in the study of ferromagnetism and is named after the pioneering work in the field. Understanding the Curie temperature helps in the development of various technologies, including magnetic storage and electronic devices.

input:
Mauro Zizza, DESIGN THINKING ASSIGNMENT    DT for psychotherapy: The role of story telling    I am an engineer and I became an specialist in Machine Design and Design Methods. For  reasons out of the scope of this essay, I became a psychotherapist in several approaches,  one of them being existential therapy.     In some point last year, I realized that I operated sometimes in therapy under the scope of  design stages and thought of having a method based on that. This is not the method itself  but a reflection on the coincidences and how a DT is affected by the method itself and, if  seen correctly, it is also a healing process for some.    What is?    When I have a new client or a regular one, I alway ask: what do you want to work with? So  they begin telling me a story. At the first stages I use the phenomenological method to keep  asking what is? Just the data, no judgements -for now. Sometimes this is enough since the  person can change his/her story by clearing the facts and is able to separate his/her own  judgements or prejudices.     What if?    In a second stage we begin to explore what others would think. So first I ask: What do you  think I think about it? Some times they are very close and others not. So I continue to ask:  What if you see things like I do? Then we can ask: what do he/she/they think about our  session? What if you ask them? What if you propose/suggest/explore what you found out  today about yourself?    What wows?    A regular question to clients is: what are your options, your wishes, alternatives? Very often  a client is locked out and see no options. Many of the idea development from this course can  be used as a game  during the session. I use many materials. Again, one option is to have  the client re write the story with two, three, four different endings.    What works?    Therapy sessions is a safe place to try out experiments. And there are hundreds of them to  inspire the client to look out and try new behaviours. Usually they “feel” what will work for  them even is not the most safe choice out there. The experiment in the wider world can fail,  but that in itself is a success since the person is trying out new versions of himself/herself.  Returning back to therapy with a “failure” is a chance to iterate in the design process and  pick up one of the ideas the client discarded or graduate the experiment and try it out again.     Story telling

The whole process is about telling a story, rewriting it, adding chapters, considering life as a  novel where a new chapter can be written, or revisiting a previous chapter and rewriting it. In  the DT course, I liked when they talked about having empathy with those involved in the  process. Well I practice the so called humanistic approach to therapy and being empathic  and teaching that to the client is central.

input:
FINAL ASSIGMENT IN DESIGN THINKING FOR INNOVATION.

I am Muhammad Shafiq and I joined healthcare industry in 2001.

I am going to discuss and share my experience in this assignment.

Challenge:

In one hospital we have introduced 4D ultrasound specifically for ANC patients.  Unfortunately we were failing to boost it as per our expectations and there was  no progression. Management decided to review the activities and planning to  redesign the marketing plan to attract target customers.

Tool selection:

(Referrence video : WEEK 4 Rossi)

We discover through survey that people are not aware about the benefits of what  we are delivering to them .So, we decided to create awareness workshop and  showing live images to them that they could understand.

For this activity we planned to use LEARNING LAUNCH Tool to achieve the goals.

We allocate one area near gynaecology department and giving handouts, lectures  and also first session of ULTRASOUND free of cost to show the difference  between traditional and 4Dultrasound. Also announced discounts on subsequent  visit in order to bring patients back to clinic.

Insights:

In this learning launch where we deliver ideal environment and deliver service to  customer results into tremendous increase in the number of patients and success  comes where patients visit in subsequent visit that could help in rating the  success rate.It also results in the morbidity rate of infants because of timely  treatment during pregnancy.  We got success and implement the same activity to help in promoting other  departments.

Aproach:(Visualiztion)

In future I would prefer to select Visualization tool because it is the best tool to  transform idea from mind to real world and the easiest and comprehensive way  to communicate whatever comes to your mind.

input:
My reflection – Storytelling

1. Challenge:

The first tool I used was the tool of Storytelling, to attract the attention of the participants,  and to introduce my topic in an interesting way, before stating the problem and the  solution. Storytelling is a kind of showing the problem from a real perspective.

2. Selection:

I select this approach usually to start talking in front of a group of participants in solution- driven projects and innovation labs, to present my idea as a creation from a real-life  situation. For example, I have used the story of a little girl which is my neighbor, who did  a great impact on the whole neighborhood to start recycling. This story improved my  concept that says: if we want to change the fruit, we have to change the root, what I mean  is: if we want a change, a new behavior in society, we have to start from the youngest  generation, which is our kids, and then, the solution will sure achieve the expected results.

3. Application:

I have started my presentation with this story:

Last Sunday, I was resting in my home, watching the news about the recent garbage  problem in our country, where I saw one of the most annoying photos that reflected a  really bad image of the behavior of the government and citizens at the same time. And I  was thinking about an effective solution as a social initiative to solve the problem.  Suddenly, someone knocked to my door, it was Federica, the 6 years old girl who’s living  in the neighborhood. She said: “Federica, from now on, you are required to keep your plastic  bottles in the vacant room at the ground floor. I will come back each week with my parents  to gather the plastic bottles to take it to the recycling center.”

What happened just made my thoughts valid and concrete. I was thinking that this is a  root problem that require a root change. Actually, the bigger generations aren’t ready to  start applying the recycling processes. In our educational system, the environmental  awareness isn’t available. This is where I decided that the solution should be starting  there, with the younger generations, the weapon of change.

Here is where my project idea came from….and I would like to tell you more about it….

4. Insight:

What I have learned from starting in a storytelling like the mentioned one, is really an  effective start and impactful from the very beginning. The attention lasted long to the final  word of my explanation. In addition, the emotional effect that a story could make is really  huge and can make all the participants agree and vote for the idea.

5. Approach:

Next time when using my technique of storytelling I will try to mention more details and  practice a more persuasive style. And I will try to give more examples from our real life.

input: The daisy is a common flower found in many gardens and fields. It is known for its bright white petals and yellow center. Daisies are often associated with innocence and purity, making them a popular choice for bouquets and floral arrangements. They thrive in sunny environments and are relatively easy to care for, making them a favorite among gardeners.

input: Tesla, born in 1856, was a visionary inventor and electrical engineer whose contributions to the development of alternating current (AC) systems revolutionized the way electricity is distributed and used. His work laid the foundation for modern electrical power systems, and his innovative ideas extended beyond electricity to include wireless communication and energy transmission. Despite facing numerous challenges and competition during his lifetime, Tesla's legacy endures, inspiring countless engineers and inventors to push the boundaries of technology.

input: Camden, located in the state of New Jersey, is a city with a rich history and a vibrant cultural scene. It is situated directly across the Delaware River from Philadelphia, making it an integral part of the Greater Philadelphia metropolitan area. Camden is home to several notable institutions, including the Adventure Aquarium and the Battleship New Jersey Museum and Memorial. The city has undergone significant redevelopment efforts in recent years, aiming to revitalize its waterfront and downtown areas. Camden's diverse community and strategic location continue to make it an important hub for commerce and culture in the region.

input:
Reflection

Name: Akhilesh Kumar

Challenge & Selection

To start this writing, I need to talk about design thinking as an enablement and a very  important quality since it allows different ideas to be developed globally

As an experience I would like to count the time I decided to sell fast food as a way to  generate extra money during quarantine

Application

First of all, I had to think that I wanted to specialize the food service, I had to debate  between burgers and fries or sandwiches and fries.

Analysing with my brother (an economist) he told me that there were many emerging  burgers stands in the sector which would be a difficult competition but very profitable  lunch since there were no burgers at a good price and with respect to sandwiches, we  analyzed the competition and realized that there are few sandwich places. It could  clearly be said that the option of sandwiches was chosen but it is not so, since there are  few restaurants of this food one can understand that they have rotation.

After having chosen the burgers, I started trying different flavors and types of pairing,  also taking into account the final price of the product. Since the idea was to get a  product that is tasty, unusual and with a fair price for customers. With this in mind, I  bought the necessary ingredients to do a tasting with my family and select the burgers  that met the requirements we were looking for.

We started selling the product, focused on the people of the north of the city and people  we knew (family/friends), our cheapest product was a Burger with fries for eleven  thousand pesos and the most expensive was a mixture of quite a few preparations that  made an exquisite flavor together with beef achieving the pairing sought above. I was  the only one who works in the kitchen since starting to sell was up to me, but that didn’t  take away the fact that my mother helped me cut certain things while I prepared other  products.

Insight & Approach

It all ended when I began to feel quite tired since the job was demanding and the kitchen  felt small for so much workload

input: In the study of ecosystems, one often observes how animals wade through shallow waters in search of food. This behavior is crucial for species that rely on aquatic environments for sustenance. By wading, these animals can access areas rich in nutrients and small prey, which are essential for their survival. Understanding the dynamics of wading can provide insights into the health of an ecosystem and the adaptability of its inhabitants.
target: In the study of ecosystems, one often observes how animals wade through shallow waters in search of food. This behavior is crucial for species that rely on aquatic environments for sustenance. input: In today's lesson, we will explore the contributions of Robin, a renowned biologist who has made significant strides in the study of marine ecosystems. Robin's research focuses on the impact of climate change on coral reefs, and their work has been instrumental in developing conservation strategies. By analyzing data from various oceanic regions, Robin has provided valuable insights into the resilience of marine life. Students are encouraged to read Robin's latest publication to gain a deeper understanding of these critical environmental issues.

input:
Jose Garcia

Storytelling

Challenge & Selection

When I was in high school, one of my teacher told me that I was a great storyteller,

in that moment I did not understand what that means. Currently I am a student of

Administration in Balatas ES (Politecnico di Milano) and I realized

that my teacher was not wrong about me.

Nowadays I am in conflict with one of my college team. The main issue is that he is

zero empathic with people and situations, that is the cause of many problems

because the decisions that we have taken is becuse that kind of thinking, affecting

the others and seeing just for our benefit.

As a lider, I am trying to manage the team in a empathic direction, is not easy

because they really do not understand a diferent point of view of themself. That is

the reason why I am practicing the storytelling.

Application

I have been using storytelling more than before, when we are discussing about a

project, I always find a moment to use this tool. I start talking about a specific

situation of the project and then I connect that situacion with a personal one. I finish

the story making a personal reflection, in that way, they can understand very well

different point of views clearer than just go in a direction.

For example, the last session that we had was the last week, we were talking about

the diagnostic needs of training. It was a very hard topic because they propossed

some agressive techniques for the collaborators, when it was my turn to speak, I

commented my story with the training and how I felt about it, I recalled to them when

we were on a similar situation and how a lot of them felt so dissgusted, in that way,

we are working to do making better deccisions focus in a humanistic approach.

Jose Garcia

Insight & Approach.

The team now are thinking about how they felt when my teacher applied to us a lot

of questionnaires, interviews, etcetera. We were really tired and at the end of the

day, the results were not useful. Detect needs for training is a hard work, specially

when you are not part of the enviroment that you want to train. We are not thinking

about questionnaires or explicit interviews, we are relly want to be part of it, known

te process, the fomal and informal groups, how to do it and see how they are doing.

I really consider that this course going to helps me to do it definitly better. Now I am

aware about a lot of tools, attitudes, etcetera. I often think about myself as a

entrepenaur, Mexico's economy is about that, and now I realized that my creativity

is a key to do it.

input: In the study of ecosystems, one often observes how animals wade through shallow waters in search of food. This behavior is particularly common among birds like herons and egrets, which rely on their long legs to navigate through the water without disturbing the surface too much. By wading, these birds can stealthily approach their prey, such as fish and amphibians, making it an effective hunting strategy.

This behavior is particularly common among birds like herons and egrets, which rely on their long legs to navigate through the water without disturbing the surface too much. By wading, these birds can stealthily approach their prey, such as fish and amphibians, making it an effective hunting strategy.

input:
Assignment Rubric - design thinking

Challenge & Selection

In 2009 my friend Luca came to me wanting to launch a brand of clothes that spoke about Maranhão (a region of northeastern Brazil). However, historically, there were only two brands that did very well in .  has a facility of exporting lifestyle and fashion for being one of the main postcards of Brazil. In Maranhão, the clothes that were manufactured were usually destined for tourists and did not have a personality. They were just "souvenirs" of the city. Knowing this I talked with Luca and suggested the application of some techniques of Design Thinking. Initially, we needed to understand the problem from the point of view of our consumer (empathy), and for this we began to look at which brands our target public most liked to use and interact with them to delve into the motives that made them buy these products. We realized that our target audience looked a lot like us and we consumed the same brands from other locations in Brazil. Knowing this we already had an idea of  how to start to validate the idea by doing a benchmarking with these brands that we consumed.

Application

As the goal was to launch a brand that spoke of Maranhao, it was natural to start talking about how Maranhão came about, who we were colonized, what people invaded us, and what heritage these people left us. After a very deep research on the history of Maranhão, the ﬁrst collection of the brand with prints of some postcards of the state appeared in stylized form, taking as references the type of design of the brands that we used and put to sell. It was a success!!!

Insight & Approach

With this we discovered that people loved to wear clothes that showed pride in being from the region and still did not do it because they did not have a more fashionable option. We took advantage of this insight and used this approach to bring our public even closer to the process of manufacturing clothes and stamping some features that only the people who lived in Maranhao knew and liked to be identiﬁed by such characteristics. In this way, the collections have a very strong word of mouth, without the need for large investments in advertising, because the customers themselves make a point of advertasing. Today I also work in a large shopping mall and will use the design thinking to redirect the way of communicating that the mall uses today. After this course, I learned some techniques and application steps of design thinking and especially the strength of storytelling to delight consumer. -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

Última modiﬁcação:  21 de abr de 2019

input: In the art class, the students learned various techniques to improve their drawing skills. One of the exercises involved using charcoal to create depth and texture. As they practiced, each student drew a still life arrangement of fruits and vases. The instructor emphasized the importance of observing light and shadow, which helped the students enhance their compositions. By the end of the session, everyone had a better understanding of how to bring their drawings to life.

input:
Example Reflection - Storytelling - Jose Brown    Challenge & Selection  Storytelling can be a great communication tool to utilize. You can be  presented with a project or task and drill down to the actions items but if you  know the “why” behind the actions it can make all the difference in a  successful project.     I am in the healthcare industry and I work with IT on various projects and  enhancements. A recent project request from our OBGYN team to reduce  delays in getting critical report results for high risk pregnancies back to the  physician so they could take needed action. The current manual process  included faxing results and many times took hours or even a day before the  physician would see those results. This could add to an already risky situation.     This project seemed straightforward and the benefits obvious to everyone on  the team. But twelve months into the project and we could not get a  successful test completed. Many factors contributed to the stalemate. We did  not have a test environment to utilize and we had to use the production  environment to test. We experienced turnover in key roles, COVID 19 delays,  restricted physician availability and several other factors that added to the  delay.       Application  I opted to create a process flow to assist with testing so we could visualize  who was responsible for a specific task. We scheduled a meeting and opened  with a specific patient story that illustrated the risks these delayed reports can  result in without the change. Others on the call opened up about examples  they have experienced. We discussed the length of time this request has been  in process (many months now) and opened up the forum for ideas on how  best to tackle these delays. Many heads with valuable expertise are better  than my head and my limited experience with this report system. The visual  storyboard opened up the conversation and we are able to visually see how  many teams and individuals are involved in this process.

Insight & Approach  The results are still in the works. We are working through restricted schedules  with testers and physicians. But the team is more open and responsive and I  have a good feeling we will get this project completed much sooner than if we  had not included the “why” this change is so important and the impact it has  on our patients and internal customers.

input: Savannah, located in the state of Georgia, is known for its charming historic district and beautiful coastal landscapes. The city is famous for its well-preserved architecture and rich history, attracting tourists from all over the world. Visitors can explore the cobblestone streets, enjoy the vibrant arts scene, and relax in the numerous parks and squares that dot the city. Savannah's unique blend of Southern hospitality and cultural heritage makes it a must-visit destination for anyone interested in American history and culture.

input:
Reflection – Learning Launch

Francisco Ferreira

Challenge

I take part of a social enterprenuership group in my university. We were in contact

with Capão das Antas, a rural Community in the suburbs of São Carlos (SP-Brazil). We had the  intention to criate, with the local producers, a project that wold improve the quality of life in  the neighborhood. For that, in the first two months we begin to listen each farmer of Capão  and try identify the principal issues of the place and their residentes. After some visits we  concluded that the principal problem was selling the products that they plant in the local  farms. Therefore, we had the objective to make and test a business model that would increase  their rent.

Selection

In order to build a sustainable business model with the farmers, we made a first test

were we delivered the products at the costumer’s door. The action had a good result but we  want to increase even more ours sales. So we used Learning Launchs to test new ideais and  then improve our business model.

Application

After we analise all the data we had and build the business model, Its was far from be

the most sustainable and profitable one. To improve our project, we made a plan to test new  ways of seling. So every week, after we analise all the process from the farm to the costumer  door, we propose and teste the improvement in the current business model.

In order to give examples of how it works, after some weeks we notice that the

farmers were having problems in separate the orders. The costumers were ordering with na  app called whatsapp, but the person who was responsable for taking note of the orders was  messy with the conversations feed. As soon as we notice this problem we proposed a new app  that automatically cluster the orders in a simple feed. Therefore we tested this improvement  in the next week and got zero mistakes in separating the orders.

Insights

The application of Learning Launches showed me how testing is importante in building

a solid business model. Therefore, I learned that the more you test possibles ideias, more  chance of sucess you have. Testing a lot of improvement and ideas is not the fastest way to  achieve a better scenario but is cleary a certainly strategy to get there.

Approach

During all the improvement plan that we act every week we propose changes based on

what we were seeing during the deliveries. I think that we could have achieved better results  by analysis more sources of data, like feedback from all the stakeholders. Besides that we  could have made a better knowledge management of the changes proposed for the business  model.

input: The Dakota region, known for its vast plains and rich history, offers a unique glimpse into the heart of America's agricultural and cultural development. This area, encompassing both North and South Dakota, is characterized by its rolling hills, fertile farmland, and significant Native American heritage. Visitors to Dakota can explore the Badlands, a striking landscape of eroded buttes and pinnacles, or visit the historic sites that tell the story of the region's past. The Dakota region is also home to a variety of wildlife, making it a popular destination for nature enthusiasts and photographers alike.

The Dakota region is also home to a variety of wildlife, making it a popular destination for nature enthusiasts and photographers alike.

input: In the realm of contemporary art, Brooklyn has emerged as a significant figure, known for their innovative approach to mixed media. Brooklyn's work often explores the intersection of technology and human experience, challenging viewers to reconsider the role of digital media in modern society. Through a series of exhibitions, Brooklyn has captivated audiences with installations that blend traditional art forms with cutting-edge technology. Their unique perspective and dedication to pushing boundaries have earned Brooklyn a place among the most influential artists of their generation.

input: The robin is a small bird known for its distinctive red breast and melodious song. It is often seen in gardens and parks, hopping around in search of worms and insects. Robins are known to be territorial, especially during the breeding season, when they sing to defend their area. They are a common sight in many parts of the world and are often associated with the arrival of spring.

input:
Giuseppe Alberti – Design thinking for innovation

Challenge

I’m a professional photographer, based in Brazil, and weddings are my

main niche. Through this course I’ve learned some tools and methods, and now

I’m able to put in practice some ideas that lived in my mind for the last year.

As I talk to my clients, I hear some frequent problems that brides go

through, when planning their weddings – things that go from finding the right

professionals to the way that a ceremony must happen.

Selection/Application/Insight/Approach

Based on that, my idea was to develop a course, so that the brides can be

more prepared to the problems that appear during a wedding plan.

And here enters the design thinking: I’ve interviewed some brides, being

or not my clients, to have a deeper understand of the issues that they go and

gone by.

Focusing on the problems of them, I’ve learned about the core pain they

face, and confirmed my assumption, that the course will solve some serious

problems. I will now proceed and test ways to do. If it becomes necessary,

pivoting and looking for alternative paths to solve the problems I hear about so

often.

or not my clients, to have a deeper understand of the issues that they go and

gone by.

problems. I will now proceed and test ways to do. If it becomes necessary,

pivoting and looking for alternative paths to solve the problems I hear about so

often.

input:
Kashi Papa

Example Reflection – Storytelling tool.

Challenge and Selection

I am an SME Account Manager for a big telecommunication company. My organization every year  (before the pandemic) organize a weekend for all B2B sales employees including back-office. We are  gathering in a hotel for an “all-together weekend”. During this weekend a lot of activities take place.  One of the activities, which includes bonding activities like games, is brainstorming in order to make  create ideas for the organizations for future launches.

My team decided a few years ago during one of these weekends, to ask from the Organizations to  create a software tool which every Account Manager would have at his tablet and when he visits any  client would have in real time all the information that he needs about the client phone lines. He could  also use this application to create new contract for new phone lines of existing customers.

The tool we used to make our proposition is Storytelling. We need as much as possible information  about this software tool.

Application

During this weekend time we discussed with all the account managers about their needs explaining  to them that addressing their needs could have a huge impact for all of us. We discussed about every  day activities and how this tool would improve the meetings with the clients. We also contacted with  some clients and asked them about their opinion, what kind of information they want from their  account manager to have with him during their meeting. We also discussed with senior managers  about the privacy issues and what information would the organizations allowed us to have in real  time during client meetings. Everyone helped us and our proposition prepared.

Insight

During the us of the Storytelling tool we identified our assumptions. Other Account managers did  things with a different way as we did, they also spend different amount of time with every client. We  also find out that some clients needs more information and more quality meetings with them. We  also find out that the time we (the account manager) would spend with every customer would highly  increase and so less clients meetings would take place every day. The more important issue that  appeared is the privacy issues that appeared from the organization. Moving forward our team review  the key issues and in the final propositions we included all the information that we find out.

Approach

A lot of information appeared using storytelling tool and our proposal was a really good one as the  organization also admitted. After software would be launched by the IT department we could also  use the Learning launch tool in order to improve the software and to add more information on it. I  think that this course gave me an excellent opportunity to learn improved techniques and think things  with a different way.

input: Hi my name is Silvia, I am a student at  the IIT. First, I would like  to talk about a little bit of mind mapping.  Mind mapping is one of the most powerful tools  in the design thinking toolbox. it's used to look for patterns, it's used to look for insights,  that lead to more creative solutions to your design challenge.      Mind mapping is a tool that I use most of the time in my school, and it has helped me to  make my projects look positive in a creative way. Right now, I am a student at the  IIT (located in Mexico), and I carry out subjects in which they make  us interact with other people doing teamwork. Practically most of the time I spend  interacting with people, talking about problems, and we always try to solve them by giving  each our point of view, and finally arrive with the best solution and the best idea.  However, this whole process has not been easy, and thanks to the Mind Mapping tool it  has helped us to organize our ideas and to better understand the situation.    I have a story specifically in which we use these kinds of tools, this was in a subject called  "reflection project," in which we had to gather several people to think about a problem  that people with disabilities suffer, and makes it difficult for them to enter a public place  specifically in a hotel.     We got some ideas on how to solve this problem, however our ideas were many and not  very specific, but thanks to the Mind Mapping tool we were able to organize more. Since  each of us had different ideas, we had to expose them in an innovative way ,which by this  means (using Mind Mapping) the other colleagues were able to understand with what we  wanted to understand , that is, the possible solutions to the problem . Finally,this is a type  of tools more visual than conceptual , therefore also the project we chose could be better  understood.    The ideas we put forward were different as well as making a mechanism of escalators so  that people with this disability could move from one place to another easily ,We also had  the idea of making an emergency bracelet with which I had the purpose that with a sensor  , they inside the hotel could contact those who work in the hotel and so be able to locate  them by means of the device ,and thus attend to them in what they need. Finally thanks  to this tool we were also able to use it for the results we obtained when making our  prototype.    It also helped us a lot to organize our results that we got from doing the hotel survey, and  so they told us their opinion about this project. Finally, I can say that even people outside  our environment made us some comments, which many of them concluded that thanks to  our organization , innovation, and creativity (by using a Mind Map), they were able to  better understand the project, and the results were successful.

input:
John Estrada

December 6, 2021

Design Thinking Reflection

Mind Mapping

Challenge:  Creating a comprehensive multi-year strategic plan that captures new value- driven company goals and objectives that allow for business growth and  innovation is a difficult task. The level of complexity increases when prioritizing  which objectives offer the most value while minimizing risk; based on stakeholder  input, market data, employee feedback, and past lessons learned.

Selection:  To better understand the individual business components (gaps, past targets, key  performance indicators, etc) and align them with the collective stakeholder input  (data, key lessons etc) I selected mind mapping as my preferred tool.

Application:  Mind mapping was instrumental in my ideation sessions held over several days  with stakeholders across my company. Each session was 2 hrs long (3 sessions  total) with key stakeholders participating in all 3 sessions. The goal of my  ideation exercises was to:   1. Level 0: Start with the organization’s mission, vision, value at the center circle

2. Level 1: Itemize the various departments/business units that are parts of the  organization as a whole

3. Level 2: Create the following groups for each department: 1. Staff 2.  Technology 3. Process

4. Level 3: Create the following categories: 1. Challenges 2. Successes 3.  Future Goals then place stakeholder input, data, insight within each bucket

5. Draw connector lines for all dependencies and/or similarities between  departments

Insight:  Having the ability to visualize the various components of my organization and  showing how each department and their challenges, successes, and goals were  directly connected gave all stakeholders that participated in the exercise much  more clarity on the current-state as well as where they needed to be for the  future-state. The mind map made prioritizing the future roadmap less complicated  since all stakeholders were onboard. We all knew we were on the same bus and  finally had a map with a pin for our destination.

Approach:  I would not change my approach. Humans are visual creatures that identify  patterns naturally, that makes mind mapping an outstanding tool that can both  capture input from lots of stakeholders in real-time and connect all components  of a challenge at a 50k ft view down to a 1k ft view.

input: In the world of economics, the term "rich" often refers to individuals or entities possessing substantial wealth or assets. This wealth can be measured in various forms, such as cash, real estate, or investments. The concept of being rich is subjective and can vary greatly depending on the societal and cultural context. For instance, what is considered rich in one country might be seen as merely comfortable in another. Understanding the dynamics of wealth distribution is crucial for analyzing economic policies and their impact on different segments of the population.

input:
Mind Mapping - The case of a facilitated mobile selling platform.

Author: Yunier Leung

In 2011 a colleague and I embarked in a difﬁcult task, building the most advanced

craft beer e-commerce that our market had ever seen. Having worked together in the  largest craft beer company in Latin America, we had the experience and knew we could  do better. During the design process of the new business, we saw ourselves locked in a  sea of technical options on how the application could be built, what it would have and  what not. We collected about 200 features up to then, and they should be narrowed to a  maximum of a handful manageable items. But how?

The selection and application of the tool happened concomitantly. As we laid out so

many disconnected options in front of us, so we began organizing and connecting the  dots. Soon enough, the tool showed itself to us: Mind Mapping. It is extremely useful  exactly to that: surface patterns in a large knowledge base. The tool consists of  reorganizing the ideas hierarchically, starting from a initial, broad and general point. In our  case, this was the ﬁnal result: an app. Soon some clusters and a workﬂow surfaced, lot's  of correlated small pieces falling into larger ones, as steps of a larger construction.

After some days of work, the ﬁrst insight appeared: we just needed the steps really

useful to take products from our stock to the client, exactly how a well done mind map  should look, organized and sensible. Everything that stepped in the way of our goal should  and could be removed. We ditched away collecting too much information from our clients,  we built stocks near them, with really fast deliveries, removing the distance itself. Added  artiﬁcial intelligence algorithms to predict lot's of information that was not central to the  user experience, but that would facilitate the conversion. Lot's of features were  consolidated in small, really needed groups.

The approach turned out to be very efﬁcient. When we reached the prototype

version of the business it was elegant, simple and goal centric. In every new phase of the  business we reapplied designing mind maps so that everyone knew both processes,  hierarchies and what really matter: how everything connected. I could not change anything  beyond applying the tool earlier every time.
target:
Mind Mapping - The case of a facilitated mobile selling platform.

input:
Reflection - Storytelling

Pietro Gabrielli

Challenge & Selection

In this course of Design Thinking I learned the basics about several design tools. One of those is storytelling. Storytelling is my favorite because it is so simple and easy to apply. It involves presenting your product or business in a story format that truly intrigues the potential customer or investor; while also creating a cause for a problem and making a list about different ways to solve it.

Application

Most likely, each and everyone of those reading this have gone for some shopping in some kind of clothing store and used a fitting room at least once in their lives. If you have used a fitting room several times, you must have felt that stress of not knowing when the employee might come knock on your door and open it because he couldn't hear you reply "taken" for some reason or another. Maybe the music inside the store was too loud, maybe you just panic and don't know what to do or say, or perhaps you happen to be deaf and/or mute. So, what happens next? The employee opens the door and there you are with barely any clothes on and some other customers catch a glimpse of you almost naked. If you go shopping regularly, then you must know the fear that I'm talking about.

While I was shopping myself I wondered: what if I were to design a device (either automatic or manual) that lets the employee know whether someone is currently using the fitting room or not?

Insight & Approach

My audience in this case, would be the customers that go shopping in clothing stores regularly, as well as investors and stores’ owners.

To apply this tool properly, it’s important to tell the story in the most intriguing way to the people that truly care about such things. After building a team for this presentation, we would need to be constantly asking relevant questions to our audience and then providing the answers to those questions. One of those questions could be: “How could this affect the overall customer’s experience apart from just using the fitting room?”. In that case, we can assure that the customer’s experience will be better, even if they don’t need to use the fitting room. Why? Because the employees’ workload will become less than usual, and that means they’ll be able to attend other tasks that are just as necessary, such as looking for a certain size for a customer, attending the cash register, welcoming customers into the store, and so on.

input: Challenge & Selection     “You have always been good in storytelling” my friends and family said to me in last ten years. I  can tell about my dream like a novel or a movie. They like listening to me. I must confest that I  like telling. Even my anecdotes amused them. I know it is about how to tell more than what you  tell : )  I have lots of stories about this but I will tell you the last one.       Application   My friend, Mohammed, wanted to learn German language but he always feared to give up early. He had not no  time to go to a course. I recommend him a software “Rosetta Stone”. He couldn’t believe because a  software cannot achieve this. I needed a story to manipulate him. I knew the programme and I really  knew how beneficial and functional it is. I said “Mohammed, this is amazing pogramme. You have ability to learn a  language. I had a friend who wants to Yiddish language to live in Israel. And I think it is more difficult  than German language I think. He started to learn from Rosetta Stone and got some books, and got  some friends on web. He couldn’t believe how he proceed. And do you know what? He lives in Tel Aviv  now. But in real I had no friend like him. Nobody wanted to learn Yiddish and lived in Israel : )      Insight & Approach     I was sure my insight was true. He just need a encouragement. Mohammed doesn’t still know the truth about  Yiddish. But Mohammed can speak German in intermediate level. Sometimes we just need a mini spark to flare.  And a good storytelling can do it easily.

input: In the realm of literature, being frank is often a crucial element for character development. Authors use frank dialogue to reveal the true nature of their characters, allowing readers to connect with them on a deeper level. This technique can be seen in various genres, where characters who are frank about their feelings and intentions often drive the narrative forward.
target: In the realm of literature, being frank is often a crucial element for character development. input: The melody of a song is its most recognizable element, often carrying the emotional weight and thematic essence of the piece. In music theory, a melody is defined as a sequence of notes that are perceived as a single entity. It is the part of the music that people often hum or sing along to, and it can vary greatly in complexity and style. Understanding how melodies are constructed can enhance one's appreciation of music and improve one's ability to create music.

input: In the world of science, few names are as revered as that of Marie Curie. Her groundbreaking research on radioactivity not only earned her two Nobel Prizes but also paved the way for future discoveries in physics and chemistry. Born in Warsaw, Marie moved to Paris to pursue her education, where she met her husband, Pierre Curie. Together, they formed a formidable scientific partnership, with Pierre supporting Marie in her quest for knowledge. Their dedication to science and their contributions to humanity remain an inspiration to many.

input: Autumn is a season characterized by the transition from summer to winter. It is marked by the shedding of leaves from deciduous trees, creating a colorful landscape of reds, oranges, and yellows. During this time, temperatures begin to drop, and days become shorter. Many cultures celebrate autumn with festivals and harvests, as it is a time when crops are gathered and prepared for the colder months ahead.

Coursera Challenge

MINDMAP

AGILE Implementation Challenges as a design thinking Mindmap

Student- Shujon Rohman

The Assignment Rubric

This Coursera assignment depicts the usage of Mindmapping as a tool for design thinking for a problem – AGILE Implementation Challenges

Core Challenges in

Implementing AGILE in a

Matrix Organization

Challenge –

Implementation of AGILE

practices in a Matrix

organization

Rational – Misconceptions

about AGILE are prevalent

in a traditional

organization. Employees

have different mind

blockages. This exercise

helps to understand those

and come up with a

strategy

Selection – In our highly

matrixed organization,

with traditional IT,

changing mindset is

difficult

Application – We did this

exercise to understand the

key concerns, like adapting

to change, availability of

business for quick POC and

so on. It was a session with

business and IT leaders

Insight – Every

participant were asked to

state the issue with AGILE

manifesto, it was mapped

to central idea.

How we Did it? –

Assimilate the central and

tertiary ideas and map

them. Look at

correlations and concerns

and address them

Wastage of Money

Long Term

MINDMAP- AGILE implementation Challenges

Being AGILE

What is

What WOWS

What if

What Works

The Outcome

Mindmapping lead the team to understand the issues and come up with a solution approach

01

WHAT IS

02

WHAT IF

03

WHAT WOWS START

04

WHAT WORKS

WHAT IS

In this part the teams mind mapped the main

issues. The central of that being business was not

accepting change, which was due to risk in the

well established ways of working, inducing more

cost and time to perform the work.

WHAT IF

In this section we presented the team with a

comparison in a brainstorming session as to what

will it look like if we had new softwaresvwhich

allowed to save time and money. Also the

business did a competitive analysis of where they

are falling behind in the market and come up with

a roadmap.

WHAT WOWS

While immediate changes to softwares and

upgrades seemed a great idea along with

automation, but disrupting the business had

customer impacts leading to legal and

compliance impacts

WHAT WORKS

A systematic planned replacement / upgrade of

the softwares, with proper OCM along with a IT

and Business joint team to look at each

implementation problem will help getting the

teams on board. Also leadership level change was

desired

input:
Example Reflection – Storytelling

Challenge & Selection

Storytelling is an important design thinking tool. I use it to work with youth in difficulty in a suburben  area of Tunis. Those teenagers have communication difficulties, are aggressive, some of them live in  very difficult situations in families with alcohoolic or unemployed fathers that are violent with them  by assault or rape. Some of them quit their schools and become drug dealers or thieves. These  teenagers need to be empowered by culture, education and art . They need to be more confident in  their future and welth. I spend a lot of time finding ways to improve their circumstances and help  them become good and happy citizens. I work with them helped by some students from different  profiles : art students, ingeneers… We share with those youth stories about our lives to give them  more confidence and help them change ideas about life. We use stories to better understand their  fears, needs and interests.  Those stories help me convince local responsables in the community to  give those youth some facilities to do activities helping them well being.

Application

I used story telling with local authorities to get help and more involvment in the protection of these  youth in difficulty. Telling a story of a 13 years old boy Ashley who quitted his family house and lives in  the street. Ashley was used to be beated by his father every day. He decided not to return to his home  and to live in the street . He became a thief. I worked with Ashley and helped him express his fears and  calm his agressivity with art. Ashley changed his vision to the society and became more creative and  confident. He had the courage to face his family problem and returned back his home. Ashley is helping  me supervise other teenagers in difficulty and reduce agressions and violence in the community.

I also used story telling with those youth in difficulty to approach them and let them be more  confident to tell their own stories and describe their problems and fears.

Insight & Approach

Ashley’s story convinced the local authorities to invest in more cultural spaces and activities to supervise  youth in difficulty in the community. They became more aware of the importance of supervising  those teenagers.

Design thinking helps me have more structured ideas and take the best decisions for my actions. To  help teenagers in difficulty, design thinking is also a good tool for them, to make them increase their  leardership and empower their creativity.

Thanks to this course, I know more about the importance of storytelling when presenting my ideas. I  will use more this tool because it enables and allows easy contact and a better transmission of my  ideas.

input:
Learning Launch Reflection

Rosa Perez

Challenge

Visvesvaraya

Technological University, family entrepreneurship brand focused on selling healthy desserts for  diabetics, vegans and healthy life searchers began operations at home. The challenge is being  known as an alternative way to enjoy delicious desserts sugar less and carbohydrates less but not  just for an event, we desire that a family could buy one cake every week.  The brand must be  known for a delicious taste and the best buy and delivery process, using technology and social  networks.

According to best innovation practices, we had some recipes using healthy ingredients and we had  some baking instruments but we didn´t know about the taste of the cake using stevia, panela and  oat. We need to know successful recipes, and listen people with experience in baking, selling and  delivering desserts. The best way to increase our knowledge was participating in baking classes  and participating in focus groups with experimented people.

Selection

We use learning launch like design thinking tool to prepare a cake prototype, we inspired on “truth  confirms assumptions about recipes, presentation and delivery process”. Every Sunday all team  members worked together in the process, we began with a chocolate cake recipe and at the end,  we tasted and expressed opinion about presentation, time spent and taste.

Application

First Learning launch, in the baking lab, the chocolate cake had good taste but the presentation  was terrible, our experience wasn´t enough to have a perfect product and we needed more  instruments like a mixer for example.

Second Learning launch, next week, the team agreed with taste and presentation but we needed  more products to offer, what happen with chocolate haters? Healthy pastry classes were perfect  to find successful recipes and a designer help us with the logo (brand identity).

Insight & Approach

We decided the timeframe (a week), cheap investment (ingredients and pastry classes) and it let  us knew that we must offer three products and detailed delivery conditions before the sale  agreement. The first market was our family members (with skeptics) and after we sale cakes on  demand. We offer different flavors and we have loyal clients.

input:
REFLECTION

VISUALIZATION

By Melissa Martinez

Challenge

I am a university student in the last semesters of Administration. Last semester I took a course on  Development of Creative Potential. Throughout this class we addressed different creativity  techniques and for the final evaluation we were asked to do a work as a team in which it was  necessary to design a creativity development program as if we were going to implement it in a real  company.

My team and I develop the points of the work and prepare it for a first review. Our teacher put  particular emphasis on that our projects looked 100% school-based and that we should make them  look professional, like a formal company would present a program in real life. My colleagues seemed  quite disoriented, they didn't know how to make our work look professional. I offered to do it and  tried to explain the idea to them.

Selection

I am not a professional nor do I have much experience in the business world of work. However, I am  a mostly visual person and I like to work with these elements.

As mentioned in the video conference that talks about this tool, visualization is quite important in  our way of communicating. By having to use the right side of our brain (often ignored) we can think  non-verbally and be more creative. That is why I chose this tool, because the impact that an element  has on our view is what will influence it to attract our attention or go unnoticed.

Application

The first thing I did was a quick evaluation of the job we had, it was pretty ugly and it didn't attract  attention at all. Right away, I myself visualized in my mind how I would like it to look and feel  professional, creative, and impactful.

Then I looked for graphic elements of all kinds to highlight my document and applied them:  headings, images, graphics and figures, color combination, letters, emphasis and distribution of the  text, etc. The result was amazing even for me, it was truly professional and eye-catching work.

Insight

I discovered the enormous power of visualization, even when I already knew its importance. He  encouraged me to submit professional and carefully done work beyond written information. It also  took me out of my creative limits and out of my common thinking. Now I think that it is an important  element in design thinking and that I can always improve.

Approach

I would like to keep improving this tool for my own use and to help others as well. I would implement  other tools to expand and improve my ideas and my teamwork, creating innovative or different  things than usual. I would go through the entire design thinking process or use tools that I acquired  in my class.

input: The Kelvin scale is a thermodynamic temperature scale where absolute zero, the point at which all molecular motion ceases, is zero Kelvin. Unlike the Celsius and Fahrenheit scales, Kelvin does not use degrees. It is widely used in scientific contexts because it provides an absolute reference point for temperature measurements. The Kelvin scale is essential in fields such as physics and chemistry, where precise temperature measurements are crucial.

input:
Maribel Rodriguez   Reflection Paper

I am currently taking my Thesis and I am having a hard time choosing a topic or a problem. In

this case, I decided to use the mind mapping tool. I started by brainstorming topics that I am interested  in. Then, I particularized by creating sub-topics. By doing so, it allowed me to organize my ideas and  concepts visually.

Mind mapping helps me recall and study my ideas easier. It lessens the complications of my

design thinking process. It shows the topics I am most and least interested in. It gives my thoughts a  structure and helps me generate new ideas.

Also, when I am conflicted between two concepts, mind mapping helps me analyze and

compare all related factors so I can make the right decision.
