input:
Reflection – Storytelling

Challenge

My name is Alberto and I’m a middle manager (one of many directors) of a Technology practice in a large  consulting company. I play the role of a project manager (or sometimes team lead) in the  implementation of complex corporate IT-systems, such as an ERP system or a corporate Data  Warehouse / Business Intelligence system. My clients are big companies, usually leaders on local  market. They work in different industries – oil & gas, metals & mining, coal production, power  generation and distribution, agriculture and so on, – but often have typical / similar problems and  requirements, so my experience is relevant to many of them.

I’m basically a technical (IT) specialist, dedicated to ‘delivery’ – system design and implementation. Sales  and marketing are not my favorite skills. But over the past three years I have been responsible for  developing an interesting complex functionality (Commodity Trading application) for a large coal  producer, and it makes sense to bring this accumulated experience to the market (to the C-level  management in the relevant companies).

Selection

Storytelling looks like the right technique for promotion of our skills and experience in CTRM  (Commodity Trade and Risk Management). My project team, together with the client, has come a long  way, made some mistakes (not fatal), learned many lessons and finally achieved good results, so I can  tell some interesting stories based on it.

Application

I have prepared two types of presentations about our project and CTRM experience:

•  Long story with a description of our client, business objectives, project approach, technical  architecture and many details and examples. It was addressed to a rather narrow audience – for  example, the participants of the metals & mining conference.

•  A more general and shorter presentation intended for a much wider audience– just description  of base problems, typical client requirements and issues, and outline of the possible system  architectures.

Insight & Approach

Then based on these materials (combination of detail comprehensive project description and kind of  elevator pitch about CTRM) I was ready for any scenario – long story for experts, short introduction for  newcomers and so on. But even very short story, to be effective, requires tones of background  preparation – analysis of target audience, development of the logical structure of the narrative, a  selection of striking examples from real life and … endless training.
target:
Reflection – Storytelling

Challenge

My name is ⟨NAME⟩Alberto⟨/NAME⟩ and I’m a middle manager (one of many directors) of a Technology practice in a large  consulting company. I play the role of a project manager (or sometimes team lead) in the  implementation of complex corporate IT-systems, such as an ERP system or a corporate Data  Warehouse / Business Intelligence system. My clients are big companies, usually leaders on local  market. They work in different industries – oil & gas, metals & mining, coal production, power  generation and distribution, agriculture and so on, – but often have typical / similar problems and  requirements, so my experience is relevant to many of them.

I’m basically a technical (IT) specialist, dedicated to ‘delivery’ – system design and implementation. Sales  and marketing are not my favorite skills. But over the past three years I have been responsible for  developing an interesting complex functionality (Commodity Trading application) for a large coal  producer, and it makes sense to bring this accumulated experience to the market (to the C-level  management in the relevant companies).

Selection

Storytelling looks like the right technique for promotion of our skills and experience in CTRM  (Commodity Trade and Risk Management). My project team, together with the client, has come a long  way, made some mistakes (not fatal), learned many lessons and finally achieved good results, so I can  tell some interesting stories based on it.

Application

I have prepared two types of presentations about our project and CTRM experience:

•  Long story with a description of our client, business objectives, project approach, technical  architecture and many details and examples. It was addressed to a rather narrow audience – for  example, the participants of the metals & mining conference.

•  A more general and shorter presentation intended for a much wider audience– just description  of base problems, typical client requirements and issues, and outline of the possible system  architectures.

Insight & Approach

Then based on these materials (combination of detail comprehensive project description and kind of  elevator pitch about CTRM) I was ready for any scenario – long story for experts, short introduction for  newcomers and so on. But even very short story, to be effective, requires tones of background  preparation – analysis of target audience, development of the logical structure of the narrative, a  selection of striking examples from real life and … endless training.

input: In my journey through high school, I was fortunate to have the guidance of my history teacher, Mr. Thompson. His passion for teaching and his dedication to his students were truly inspiring. I remember one particular lesson where Mr. Thompson brought history to life by organizing a mock debate on the American Revolution. His ability to engage us in such interactive activities made learning not only informative but also enjoyable. I often think back to those classes and how Mr. Thompson encouraged us to think critically and express our ideas confidently.

input:
Design Thinking Assignment - Reflection

Dieter Berger

Challenge

My challenge is to convince people to reduce their organic waste in their houses

by descomposting it. We are developing an electrical domestic composter which is able

to reduce the organic waste, generated in the kitchen, in between 60% and 80% and it

takes no more than 72 hours. The compost generated by our device has got a lot of

nutrients and can be used for growing plants and trees. Our challenge is to create the

habit of decomposting organic waste in every house we can, and show that it can be

extremely important for our planet and vey handful to use on gardens and organic food

growers.

Selection

Storytelling is the chosen tool. It is able to cause a really good impact on the

people, helping them to visualize the importance and benefits of decomposting their

organic waste at home. By adressing them individually they will be able to feel that they

are really important in this process and they are trully a part of it. We want every

individual to be inspired through the current scenario, making them feel that they are

contributing for the environment’s future by reducing their waste, and in exchange they

can get the best nutrients for their plants and maybe grow their own food. The chosen

tool can help to make everyone feel and understand the purpose of the product.

Application

By using the storytelling tool we were able to tell our customers what is the real

impact of organic waste to our planet. Showing the percentages of organic waste

generated in the last decade by humans caused a huge impact and could make them

feel responsible for it too. Bringing this responsability to them and making them to feel

as part of it gave us a big chance to introduce our product and consequently the habit

of decomposting their organic waste at home. Some of them got really excited to be

parto f something with a bigger purpose, other enjoyed a lot the idea of producing their

own compost for their organic gardens. The story generated had a very positive impact

on their lives and was able to get mosto f the desired effect.

Insight

By applying the storytelling to the adressed problem I was able to feel part of

something bigger too. It caused a big impact on myself as well, making me feel

responsible to go further and help people with their organic waste too, showing them

that every human being must care about the waste they generate and that it has a big

impact to our planet. It came to me that I must work hard to make our product accessible

to everyone.

Approach

For the next time I would use storytelling with a visualization tool! By using both

of them I am sure I would have a bigger impact. By showing to their eyes what our waste

really cause to our planet I would certainly make them feel we are in trouble if we don’t

start reducing our waste now. Sometimes one picture is worth a Thousand words.

input: Autumn is a season characterized by the transition from summer to winter. It is marked by the shedding of leaves from deciduous trees, creating a picturesque landscape of reds, oranges, and yellows. This time of year is often associated with harvest festivals and the preparation for the colder months ahead. The crisp air and shorter days signal the end of the growing season, making it a time for reflection and gratitude.
target: Autumn is a season characterized by the transition from summer to winter. It is marked by the shedding of leaves from deciduous trees, creating a picturesque landscape of reds, oranges, and yellows. This time of year is often associated with harvest festivals and the preparation for the colder months ahead. The crisp air and shorter days signal the end of the growing season, making it a time for reflection and gratitude.

input: In the study of ecosystems, it's important to understand how animals wade through water to find food. This behavior can be observed in various species, such as herons and flamingos, which often wade in shallow waters to catch fish or crustaceans. By wading, these animals can access food sources that are not available on land, demonstrating an adaptation to their environment.
target: In the study of ecosystems, it's important to understand how animals wade through water to find food. This behavior can be observed in various species, such as herons and flamingos, which often wade in shallow waters to catch fish or crustaceans. By wading, these animals can access food sources that are not available on land, demonstrating an adaptation to their environment.

input:
Diego Estrada

Design Thinking Assignment

Visualization Tool

Challenge & Selection

The elderly were having a hard time adapting to the changes we brought in our bank. As  a result of a poorly implemented linear solution, a more customer centric approach was  needed.

After learning about design thinking in this course, we decided to apply it to solve this  problem. The visualization tool allowed the team to create a dynamic presentation using  diagrams, figures and drawings on the go that really resonated among the stakeholders.  Previous to this change, none of our solutions seemed to be adequate for them, but the  new implementation created a different type of connection with them that helped them  understand the problem in the way the team and I did.

Application

The process starts in the prep time. The team uses a series of tools and software to  develop a presentation using the surveys gathered during research and the solutions we  created during the process. The use of graphs to quickly show statistics in a fully visual  way, rather than verbally was a game changer.

After having a presentation prepared, the team hands an activity to the stakeholders,  where the solutions discussed previously appear. Nonetheless, the solutions need more  work to them. After this. The stakeholders are asked to help complete the solutions  while the team and I create diagrams on a blackboard to represent how their  suggestions would impact on this specific problem.

The use of a group activity strengthens the bond between the company and their  investors. It makes them feel like they take part and help solve the problems as well as  show how customer centric the solutions are. Every complaint and suggestion from  customers are read and evaluated using the graph shown in the course (Involving: can  we do it? Can we afford it? …). The finalization of this activity leaves the team and the  stakeholders on the same page. It allows them to completely understand and feel part  of the solution and also gives them the chance to ask better questions, which eases the  work of the team.

Insight & Approach

The use of this method created a new workflow in the Design Team. It increased the  productivity and the success rate as well as the customer/stakeholders satisfaction. The  use of the visualization tool created an engaged group of people who work together to

Diego Estrada

find a solution based on their customer satisfaction. This solution is later revised and  tweaked with the help of the stakeholders who are deeply involved in the process.

Presentations, graphics, and activities have added a huge increase in satisfaction. As a  company we also learnt that engaging different areas can be difficult because of the  varying levels of understanding, but when paired with the adequate process things just  flow.

(This story is fictional and was created for solving the assignment)

input:
Reflection Tiago Borges about Mind Mapping

Challenge    The challenge chosen was around and Onboarding process in an IT Company. The idea  was apply the design thinking tools to understand how the process happened and  improve the processes using the tools. Mind mapping is a great tool inside the Design  thinking and was used to understand the gaps that the process had and how was the  things that could be improved.   The case is that when an It company hired new employees to work as analysts for  different customers, there was no an onboarding process. That process was done  thought the project culture, generating a lot of none equalizes in the answers to the  end users.    Selection    I chose the mind mapping because we haven’t sure about the process like happened  that moment and We would need to understand how was the things that could be  improved. Using the ideas learned at the course, I started the exercise with 10  employees, mapping the process like happened.   After this, we had more 2 phases, to get the pain points and the improvement ideas. In  the end of the exercise, we used the periodization matrix to choice the best  improvement ideas.    Application    The application happened using one session, with 10 employees of the company,  working as managers (different level). We had 3 hours to run the 3 phases of the  exercise, covering the process, pain point and improvement ideas.   In the end of the processes, we had 15 steps of the process like was running, 23 pain  points and 15 improvement ideas. We chose 11 ideas as measure ideas to apply in the  process. Analyzing this numbers, I believe that the process was a success, effective.     Insight      When the exercise started, I think that all participants don’t know about the  simulation, the exercise and about the ways that Design Thinking can improve the  process. I had to show how the things would work.   The presentation of Design Thinking changed the mind of the participants and I believe  that. As a result, we could experiment an hard difference in the process using the new  way, created using Design Thinking. Now, we have an IT onboarding process to the  new joiners.     Approach

I applied the tool selected, using prioritization matrix as an support tool, to help us to  decided how was the ideas that we would like to choose. We used effectively the Mind  Mapping.

input: In the world of ornithology, the jay is a fascinating bird known for its intelligence and vibrant plumage. These birds are part of the Corvidae family, which also includes crows and ravens. Jays are often recognized by their striking blue feathers and loud calls. They are highly adaptable and can be found in a variety of habitats, from forests to urban areas. Their diet is diverse, consisting of insects, seeds, and even small animals. Observing jays can provide valuable insights into avian behavior and ecology.

Jays are often recognized by their striking blue feathers and loud calls. They are highly adaptable and can be found in a variety of habitats, from forests to urban areas. Their diet is diverse, consisting of insects, seeds, and even small animals. Observing jays can provide valuable insights into avian behavior and ecology.

input: The Dakota region, known for its vast plains and rich history, has played a significant role in the development of the United States. Originally inhabited by Native American tribes, the area was later explored by European settlers. The Dakota Territory was established in 1861, eventually splitting into the states of North Dakota and South Dakota in 1889. This region is renowned for its agricultural contributions, particularly in wheat and corn production. Additionally, the Dakota Badlands offer a unique landscape that attracts tourists from around the world, eager to explore its rugged beauty and learn about its geological significance.
target: The Dakota region, known for its vast plains and rich history, has played a significant role in the development of the United States. Originally inhabited by Native American tribes, the area was later explored by European settlers. The Dakota Territory was established in 1861, eventually splitting into the states of North Dakota and South Dakota in 1889. This region is renowned for its agricultural contributions, particularly in wheat and corn production. Additionally, the Dakota Badlands offer a unique landscape that attracts tourists from around the world, eager to explore its rugged beauty and learn about its geological significance.

input: In the world of science, few names are as revered as that of Marie Curie. Her groundbreaking research on radioactivity not only earned her two Nobel Prizes but also paved the way for future discoveries in physics and chemistry. Marie Curie's dedication to her work and her perseverance in the face of adversity continue to inspire scientists around the globe. Her legacy is a testament to the power of curiosity and the pursuit of knowledge.

Her legacy is a testament to the power of curiosity and the pursuit of knowledge.

input:
Cristina Valerio

Design Tool: VISUALIZATION

Challenge & Selection

The tool I have decided to choose is Visualization, Visual Thinking as I prefer to call it.  I consider this tool can be helpful to count on the whole picture of the problem I am working on, in  the initial steps of the design thinking process. With it, I can support my effort to identify in the most  accurate basis, the pain point I intend to solve.

Visualization helps me to make the people pay attention to what is going on (What is?) by examining  what already exists. Visual thinking as a tool, guides my group, organizes it, leads the people to  discover the outcome desired.

As the first approach to the innovation process, this tool unlocks my thinking, unleashes creativity as  well as improves the way I communicate my ideas to the people I work with.

By using visual elements like charts and graphs, I provide an accessible way to see and understand  trends, outliers, and patterns in data and discover possibilities I have not seen yet.

Application

In my company, I am the manager of an area called Responsible Care. I usually work together with  my team, so this tool allows me to approach, identify, organize and communicate more effectively  the ideas within the group. The tool represents information visually, so it can be learned quickly and  easily.

I find it helpful to focus on the idea and guide my team members to the outcome I am looking for. By  this tool, I can communicate effectively the situation, and I challenge the people to explore the  problem, think possible scenarios and present prospect solutions for the outcome I am trying to  reach.

As I said above, I use charts and graphs, I can make a development of my concept and make it  visible for the group, the abstract idea becomes visible for them because it is expressed more  clearly.

Insight & Approach

With the knowledge I have gained throughout this course, I have imagined a better way of working  with my group, co-working all together to achieve more effectively the solution we are all looking for.

Cristina Valerio

new ways of working with visualization, adding posts to my initial diagram with ideas and  suggestions which arise from the discussions between the team members, grouping the ideas  according to the different criteria as a second step, and then finally vote for those which look more  representative of the solution we are seeking.

I recognize that the value added to the group by being aligned in the same direction, considering first  all the different ideas presented by each of the members, can strengthen us as a team, organize  information in a better way and keep all focused to achieve the results.

input:
Silvia Villalobos

Challenge:

There is a company which provides financial advisory to customers either in person or virtual.  Lately organisation climate has been seen decayed as result of arguments, hassles and the  lack of fraternity and cooperation among the campaign workers. The aim is improving  climate organisation to transmit unity and trust to our customer.

Selection:

Storytelling is the first tool selected, because this tool allows to connect with the audience,  to make understandable the message, and to transmit emotions. Campaign workers would  feel identified and understand the importance of tolerance and empathy.

Application and insight:

Many stories were told in various sections. Stories from previous experiences, fictional  stories, and stories told by participants created a different atmosphere due to participants  started to produce deeper relationships among them and recognize the importance of being  empathic. An important result was that meeting with clients were longer.

Approach:

The activities work in a good way, but visualization tool would work very well in a challenge  like this. Showing the way, some elements to get a harmonious atmosphere through  visualization is an option in a further situation.

His work primarily focuses on understanding how consumer behavior influences economic trends. input: The Kelvin scale is a crucial component in the field of thermodynamics, providing a scientific method for measuring temperature. Unlike the Celsius and Fahrenheit scales, Kelvin starts at absolute zero, the point at which all molecular motion ceases. This scale is essential for scientists because it allows for precise calculations in experiments involving thermal energy. The Kelvin unit is widely used in various scientific disciplines, including physics and chemistry, to ensure accuracy and consistency in temperature-related measurements.

input: In geography, a dale is a valley, typically an elongated depression between hills or mountains. The term is often used in the context of the British landscape, where dales are a common feature, particularly in regions like the Yorkshire Dales. These areas are characterized by their rolling hills, lush greenery, and often a river or stream running through the center. Dales provide a unique ecosystem that supports a variety of plant and animal life, making them important for both environmental and recreational purposes.

input: The Dakota region, known for its vast plains and rich history, offers a unique glimpse into the past. Visitors can explore the Badlands, a striking landscape of eroded buttes and pinnacles. The area is also home to Mount Rushmore, where the faces of four U.S. presidents are carved into granite. The region's history is deeply intertwined with the Native American tribes who have lived there for centuries, providing a rich cultural tapestry for those interested in learning more about the indigenous peoples of North America.

input: In the world of ornithology, the jay is a fascinating bird known for its intelligence and vibrant plumage. Jays are part of the Corvidae family, which also includes crows and ravens. These birds are often recognized by their striking blue feathers and loud calls. They are highly adaptable and can be found in a variety of habitats, from forests to urban areas. Jays are also known for their ability to mimic the calls of other birds, which can sometimes confuse predators or other species. Their diet is quite varied, including insects, seeds, and even small animals. Observing jays can provide valuable insights into avian behavior and ecology.

input:
Kamrul Javed 14/11/2018

Visualisation tool for drone development

Challenge

I own a drone solutions company and my/our challenge is to always improve our products and  services. Currently we are in the process of developing a new drone. With this we need to come  up with something new, something diﬀerent. Find our target audience and work around their  needs to create the ultimate drone.

Selection

As we are designing the drone in house it is clear to start with the visualisation tool to discover our  needs, our clients needs, designs, and put our ideas into a picture. Allowing us to put our ideas  together and think about possibilities we haven’t seen before.

Application

For our ﬁst visualisation tool we started with everyone in the team putting up their ideas on a  board in picture, word and design form. This lead to lots of discussions about diﬀerent types of  idea and brought up new ideas which we also added to the board. For the second part we took all  our ideas and started to see patterns, situations it can be used for and diﬀerent segments. Our  challenge was to pick out which to leave out and focus on whats important and doing so in a  group, everyone is diﬀerent and cant agree on everything.

Insight

By applying the visualisation tool, my team and I identiﬁed our key solutions and design ideas and  built a better understanding of where and how we move forward and create a more eﬃcient  workﬂow. The results were much better than expected and we though more broadly than we have  in previous meetings, discovering several new use cases for our drone which we will be pursuing.

Approach

This new way of discovering ideas had a big impact on the team and is deﬁnitely something we  are going to continue using in our brainstorming phase. Moving forward we will analyse all the  information we have put together and use it to generate story around it in video form and put the  customer experience and usage in focus. Then push it out to a select group and analyse the  responses. This will give us a better understanding of what the end user thinks of the product and  if theres anything we’ve missed in our design thinking process.

input:
Reflection

Fabiana Braga 2012/09/11

Challenge and Selection The tool I selected to reflect about is the Learning Launch Tool. The purpose of a learning launch is to get the truth about a situation and a market, and that is useful so you can save time and money when to test ideas/hypothesis/assumptions.

To test ideas/hypothesis/assumptions, and verify if they are really good, it is important to compare with good data (and not opinions or unfounded guesses). That is a challenge due to some risks that are evolved in the process, as such as confirmation bias (when you subconsciously only look for data that confirms your idea/hypothesis/assumption).

Application I used the approach of the Learning Launch to test a hypothesis I have created recently. I work as a financial consultant in an Advisory Firm, and my job in the project that I’m currently involved is to create and suggest to my client KPIs for the cost process in the financial area. Therefore, I was supposed to bring some KPIs that could be used by managers to support their analysis of the development of the area.

To achieve this, I created a list of KPIs that could be used, and I tested the efficiency of the KPIs using the experiment phase of the Learning Launch Tool. This was important, because I could review the formulas I have created for the KPIs, and adjust them when necessarily.

Insight As I described before, I used the Learning Launch tool and I experimented the efficiency and reviewed the formulas for the KPIs. After that I could see the errors and adjustments necessarily to be done. By doing these steps, I was able to prove that the KPIs could be used by the managers, therefore I gained trust enough to finish the work.

Approach I would definitely use the Learning Launch again to experiment another idea/hypothesis/assumption that I have created. Also, next time I would use the Learning Launch as a starting point for my thinking and actions on a project.

input:
ADNOC Classification: Internal

Design Thinking for Innovation 01 Nov 2015.

Peer-graded Assignment: Reflection

Submit by Dec 25, 11:59 PM PST

Submitted for peer reviews by Dla Xalid   Email: vmartinez@hotmail.com

Refilling fuel at ADNOC Petrol Station at Abu Dhabi City.

Challenge to avoid the Bottle neck for Rush hour, busy service providers at Pump filling  point, Full Car Parking, Crowded at Payment point.

Storytelling Tool is selected to resolve the issue of time delay and cars crowed during rush  hours at Petrol Station.

1. Collected Data for time duration and Customers service staff engagement at Petrol

Station.

2. Collected number of cars awaiting during rush hour.

Using the tool provided options to resolve the issues:

1. Once car enters petrol station to select appropriate filling pump lane.  2. Avoid rush hour and plan alternative time for refilling fuel.  3. Pay for fuel by bank Credit card.

The benefit of applying the above options while communicating with customers and Petrol  station service providers:

1. Positive Feedback from Customer stories, the Satisfaction and Happiness.  2. Able to serve customer with an efficient.  3. Enhanced timing requirement to refill fuel.  4. Win to win situation

I might use similar tool for challenging cooking and delivering Pizza to customers on time  delay after placing the order and serve hot Pizza.

input:
Reflection – Storytelling

Challenge & Selection

Storytelling is today the main tools one haves to promote your brand, product or idea. For the last  10 years, I’ve been involved in educational projects, specially focused on millennials. In order to  understand how to better communicate the information we wanted our audience to learn we asked  ourselves two questions: what millennials are interested in, and how they consume information. The  conclusions showed us that they are more willing to consume information when is given by someone  in the format of a story, for example, we saw a huge increase in Vlogers that talked about academic  issues like geography, politics and history, and they were very successful in their channels.

Application

We decided to tell our story as our main educational tool. Instead of talking about general history,  we started the conversation y telling our personal history. Before even talking about complex  realities, we talked about our personal challenges and dilemmas. Our classes became sessions and  the structure of teacher and students disappear. We were equals talking about real issues, learning  about how history could help us overcome our challenges by learning from it. Another very  important aspect is that we encouraged the participants to have a conversation with us, as  millennials are so used to do in social media, we gave them an opportunity to tell their stories.

Insight & Approach

Education is a challenging process, and with every generation we should study a better way to do it.  Sadly, formal education is less open to changes. Our results show that, when applying storytelling  techniques to education, engagement grows and students are more open to learn.

By telling our story we became humans to the participants, and they were interested in hearing  what we had to say. More interestingly, we were able to discover that they had a lot to say too and  they realized that they knew more than what they thought about the subjects we were talking about

Now, I work in an executive manager position. Until this course I was not able to see the use of this  technique to my new job. This course allowed me to better understand how I can use techniques  from different backgrounds to create more holistic plans for the nonprofit I manage.

I think design thinking, in combination with classic techniques of project management, will allow us  to create programs that adapt better to the new reality of the world.

Mauro Pacheco

input:
Design Thinking for Innovation - Mind Mapping  Juan Flores

Challenge

I’m a software engineer student at Sathyabama University and my principal goal is to improve reading interest  specially on teens, in my opinion this would cause positive changes in reading comprehension and  linguistic enrichment, adding an improvement in spelling and speaking. I am going to focus on thinking  strategies to attract people's attention especially to young people. I’ve chosen this challenge because is  important to understand and analyze what are you reading to.

Selection

I’ve chosen mind mapping because I can add ideas as many as I want without respecting a concrete order  and applicating my own best distribution maybe using techniques as brain storming, nevertheless I can  compare and analyze the ideas I’ve thought and then select the best of all.

Application

I’m going to use a kind of marketing focusing on something that attract the people which we want to  convey this message or this proposal for me I would use various resources such as talks which motivate  students reading to make such a reading a habit would then use various types of methods such as in- person lectures which give reasons to read, lectures which explain more the importance and need of  reading, these lectures would be more intuitive, more participatory, etc. One aspect on the internet we can  make videoconferences to give reasons and make dynamics to encourage reading in young people,  another would be that we can give access to online books which would be more attractive for young  people since they are used on their mobile device, another of the online forms would be through  audiobooks since some young people do not like to see letters very much because the r They produce and  listen to them anyway, we would give some attractive books such as science fiction, action, among others,  since they are the ones that are read the most and the last of my ideas would be like audio books.

Insight

By chosen mind mapping y was able to add ideas as many as I want without respecting a concrete order  and applicating my own best distribution using brain storming, I was developing, such as the lectures, I put  them in the face-to-face space and online, I also analyzed some trends as an explanation and joined them  with ideas that help attract the reader.

Approach

The use of mind map helped me a lot because I could organize my ideas and use the best of all in order to  show a proper article.

input:
Guddu Singh Reflection of Design Tool Application

Challenge and Selection:

I was working on a project and needed to communicate the magnitude of the discrepancy  between men and women in consulting. I needed to show how gender representation numbers  change over time as you progress into the higher levels of consulting. This was going to do into a  powerpoint deck that later made the case for offering unique networking opportunities for  women. I knew that, in order for the Ask later to be strongest, the audience really needed to feel  the disparity that exists at the highest levels of consulting.

I was deciding between using visualization and story-telling as a means of really connecting with  the audience and making this part of the presentation impactful. Ultimately, I decided to focus on  the Visualization tool. I chose this for a couple reasons: firstly, I thought of the saying “a picture  speaks a thousand words.” I knew that if people saw not just the numbers but the numbers  reflected in visual form, that would be more impactful. Secondly, I this tools is one I am less  comfortable with, so this also felt like a prime opportunity or practice.

Application and Approach

I ended up taking the data in Microsoft excel and spent time thinking about different ways to  visualize the data. I ended up deciding to start with showing a pie that starts out showing 50%  men and 50% women at entry level roles. I then removed respective parts of the pie as we moved  up the ranks to finally leave just 17% of the Partners were women. At the same time, the portion  of the pie that represented men keeps growing at each level up the ranks. So we start out with  parity and end up showing how the disparity looks by the end.

Insights and Future Application/Approach

I learned that the more I am able to communicate with visuals, the better the message lands with  an audience. This might sound like a really obvious point to many, but my default has always  been to use words to convey points. This exercise pushed me to invest my time in trying  something different and it was really powerful. I also realized, while I was playing around with  different visualizations, that it matters a lot HOW I choose to visualize something. I think the  most effective visualization will also be the most elegant in that the “takeaway” is clear. There  should not be confusion about what an audience member should take away.

I think I want to keep practicing and I want to collaborate with a partner in the future so that  there are different perspectives in the design phase. It also helps to think of the end goal, the  thing I want to communicate.
target:

input: Ivy is a type of climbing plant that is often found adorning the walls of buildings and gardens. Known for its lush, green leaves, ivy can provide a beautiful and natural covering for structures. It is important to manage its growth, as it can sometimes become invasive, potentially damaging the surfaces it clings to. Ivy is also used in various decorative arrangements and can be a charming addition to any landscape design.

input: In physics, Planck's constant is a fundamental quantity that plays a crucial role in quantum mechanics. It is denoted by the symbol h and is used to describe the sizes of quanta, the discrete units of energy. Planck's constant is essential in the formula E=hf, where E is energy, h is Planck's constant, and f is the frequency of the electromagnetic wave. This constant helps in understanding the behavior of particles at the atomic and subatomic levels, providing a bridge between classical and quantum physics.

It is denoted by the symbol h and is used to describe the sizes of quanta, the discrete units of energy. input: Marie Curie was a pioneering scientist known for her groundbreaking research on radioactivity. Born in Poland, she moved to France to pursue her studies and eventually became the first woman to win a Nobel Prize. Her work laid the foundation for significant advancements in both physics and chemistry. Despite facing numerous challenges as a woman in science, Marie Curie remained dedicated to her research, ultimately winning two Nobel Prizes in different scientific fields. Her legacy continues to inspire scientists around the world.

input:
MIND MAP

1. Challenge

Currently, my friends and I did a start-up about self-development applications.

Sometimes we found some difficulties in working together. For example, most of the

information comes in a non-organized, non-structured way, making it harder for me and

everyone else to absorb and utilize it. So, the way I’d phrase the challenge would be: how

can we improve our start-up by structuring information we handle in a simple, can be

visualized and knowing the relationship between that information that we have.

2. Selection

Our team uses a variety of design thinking tools, one of which is the Mind Map. Mind

Map is visually organized information that is designed to help us in collaboration and

communication.

3. Application

I use a mind map for structuring information to make it simple, can be visualized, and

knowing the relationship in a way I do organize the dozens of things that the team has to

coordinate. Every project basically contains the same items: scope (what’s in, what’s

out), timeline (dates, milestones), costs, resources (people, equipment), risks, and

assumptions. but, the hardest part of learning a new subject, is visualizing how big it is

and how its concepts and details are interconnected.

4. Insight

My insight about one of the design thinking tools, which is the mind map. I think by

making a mind map in the team, we have an idea of the performance we have been

through. In addition, we can also understand each other's ideas efficiently, the purpose of

the applications we develop is even clearer. Because the idea that we have is too big or

too complex for us to rely only on our memories. Here’s where the mind mapping helps a

lot.

5. Approach

As I am seeing the benefits of mind maps in our team, I definitely plan to continue using

the tool. But, I think that sometimes the map itself can oversimplify an idea because it

only contains a broad core not including important details. Overall, I do satisfy with the

mind map that helps me to simplify some ideas. Therefore, I confidently say that I would

like to explore more design thinking tools that will help me and my friend in developing

our small start-up.

Saadiah Jie

input: The city of Salem, located in the state of Massachusetts, is renowned for its rich history and cultural significance. Known primarily for the infamous Salem witch trials of 1692, this city has become a symbol of the dangers of mass hysteria and injustice. Visitors to Salem can explore numerous historical sites, including the Salem Witch Museum and the House of the Seven Gables, which offer insights into the city's past. Additionally, Salem's vibrant arts scene and annual Halloween celebrations attract tourists from around the world, making it a unique blend of history and modern culture.

input:
Reflection – Storytelling

Diana Vazquez

Challenge & Selection

Storytelling in my field is a tool often used to connect with people all around the world who often  share an idealization of a dream or a story itself. Everyone has a dream, many people dream about  opening their own business themselves.

I have came across a lot of small business who were opened to the public with the main goal of  connecting with people and share their story (aside the obvious side of making a business). I find  these kind of processes very noticeable among the new trend of opening Little cafés or food  service places, people frequently visit these places no just because of the producto (food and  drinks), but because of the differential experience each of these business can give them.

Application

Nowadays people use social media to reach out to a broader public. It’s a very effective tool to  continue the path of storytelling itself. For example, i like to “hunt” for new places to visit every  time on social media, watching their Instagram feed can tell me so much more about the place  before i even get the opportunity to visit it, but it also makes my curiosity grow.

There’s a little patisserie that opened 3 months ago in the city, the owner used to make bread and  cakes for other cafés, quite famous around “the scene”, but she never got to open her own  business until recently. Al lof this was discovered by a simple approach and conversation, so I  wondered, how many people could feel motivated by her story and will her story make her own  brand seem more approachable and not just a single new business in town?

Insight

Using social media as a tool for storytelling can tell me so much not only about the place or their  producto, but their story itself. Has anyone tried to interview these entrepeneurs about the  challenges and dreams they had in order to get to that place were they finally were able to open  their business to public? How many of these stories can be relatable to the public itself? It makes  the whole experience more personal and engaging before you even have to visit the place, and we  know, not everyone has the time to have a 1 to 1 talk with the owner the first time they visit their  business.

Approach

Considering using an Instagram account to share the stories about these small business to the  people of a certain city or district. To chose this type of communication tool can make the content  available to a wider public and they don’t necessarily have to access a different website to get the  story. Owners can also share their story to their peers and the abundant circle of growing the  storytelling account can fuel the engagement.

This way, the next time a new costumer visits the place, they will have a familiar feeling lingering,  as if it’s visiting a new friend.

Mind mapping is used to represent how ideas or other items are linked to a central  idea and to each other. Mind maps are used to generate, visualize, structure, and  classify ideas to look for patterns and insights that provide key design criteria.

So the analysis part of the modules the software  was really hard  to define from an analyst's perspective as their internal stakeholders or investors and  company members .

Mind maps are excellent in interviews and workshops, or as  a personal thinking tool.

•  It helps you remember and recall information , Try this little experiment and It  helps you learn new concepts

•  It's a fun way of learning and  It makes complex ideas easier to understand

input:
Kaur Rubio

Reflection: storytelling

Challenge

I am a university degree in administration, in the afternoons I work in a cafeteria  called "University ESAN" in which I have multiple functions such as customer service, food  preparation and charging; I have a colleague who has been working in the  company with which I distribute the tips for a longer time and in recent weeks I have  noticed that they have dropped and more on the days than on which I rest.

Selection

I chose the storytelling tool that is that connection between people and the  information that we share, use this tool since narration allows us to interact and tell  stories with which the two parties identify, that unite us and that lead us to a  common solution because as mentioned in video 2 called the storytelling tool  “storytelling allows you to create energy, passion and emotion about something“  and I think the most important thing is to find the way in which a story helps us to give  it strength the other.

Application and Insight

One day at work I dedicated myself to observing my colleague and noticed that  when she attended to clients she did it in a rude way so I took some time with her  and we started talking, she told me that she was very strained first because she was  under pressure. through school and secondly because she did not have the support  of her parents, with that I could understand her and I told her that I also studied and  like she was very stressed by all the homework they left me, but that I went to a  psychologist who He allowed me to realize that this was the cause of my bad mood  with others because it expressed my frustration, but with his help I managed to  organize my work and student life and with it my frustration diminished.

Later she told me that she is going to a psychologist who is helping her a lot and for  my part I try to talk to her, but more importantly to listen to her, be empathetic and  find support and identification with me in order to see that everything has a solution.

Approach

This tool allowed me to see the cause of the problem and I can even say that it  made me more empathetic by being able to understand what was happening. I  can affirm that the implementation of the tool benefited us because in the following  weeks we have seen an increase in our tips and my partner looks happier, but what  could change is that this was not only between the two but also expanded it with  my boss as one motivation towards everyone, where the entire work circle comes  together and we flow together.

⟨NAME⟩Kaur Rubio⟨/NAME⟩

input: Pearls are unique gemstones formed within the soft tissue of a living shelled mollusk. Unlike other gemstones that are mined from the earth, pearls are created by a biological process. When an irritant, such as a grain of sand, enters the mollusk, the creature secretes layers of nacre around it, eventually forming a pearl. These gems have been highly valued for their beauty and rarity throughout history. Pearls come in various colors, including white, black, and pink, and their luster and shape can vary greatly, making each one unique.

Questionnaire

➢ Does it work at home based output voltage?

■ No, as we have given the option of working on chargable batteries, dc

input current wont work.

❖ Battery

➢ Is it rechargeable or one time battery ?

■ We have given the option of rechargeable batteries.

❖ Hot

➢ Is the hot mode safe?

■ Yes, we have taken into consideration of the damage, thereby giving a

lower temperature .

■ It is for fast drying.

❖ Cold

➢ What is cold mode for?

■ It is to set your hair in the style you wanted it to be.

➢ Can it dry my hair?

■ The main purpose is sor styling and not for drying.

■ This feature is mainly for the people with long hair , for whom drying the

■ For different kinds of hair we have given hair clips to style accordingly.

➢ What is the price range?

■ Price varies from 3Thousand to 8Thousand rupees.

■ Different models have different output power levels, accordingly the

input: The daisy is a common flowering plant found in many gardens and wild areas. Known for its simple beauty, the daisy has a yellow center surrounded by white petals. It belongs to the Asteraceae family, which includes other popular flowers like sunflowers and chrysanthemums. Daisies are often associated with purity and innocence, making them a popular choice for bouquets and floral arrangements. They thrive in sunny environments and are relatively easy to care for, making them a favorite among gardeners.
target: The daisy is a common flowering plant found in many gardens and wild areas. Known for its simple beauty, the daisy has a yellow center surrounded by white petals. It belongs to the Asteraceae family, which includes other popular flowers like sunflowers and chrysanthemums. Daisies are often associated with purity and innocence, making them a popular choice for bouquets and floral arrangements. They thrive in sunny environments and are relatively easy to care for, making them a favorite among gardeners.

input: The contributions of Faraday to the field of electromagnetism are monumental. Faraday, a self-taught scientist, discovered electromagnetic induction, which laid the groundwork for the development of electric generators and transformers. His experiments and lectures made complex scientific concepts accessible to the public, and his work continues to influence modern technology. Despite having little formal education, Faraday's curiosity and dedication to science led to groundbreaking discoveries that have had a lasting impact on the world.

input:
Date of submission: 30 October 2021

Author: Mdjakir Puerta

Design Thinking for Innovation – Coursera

Assignment

Mind Mapping

Challenge: In the Organization that I am working in, which is a highly regulated Pharma Industry, we  have several procedures and standards that need to be applied while implementing or developing a  computerized system. It has been a challenge for associates to know all the standards, regulations and  procedures, visualize them all at one place and apply them appropriately based on different scenarios.  Using Design Thinking and Mind Mapping tool technique tried to resolve this challenge by providing a  mind map of regulations, standards and procedures at one place in a single frame or window for  associates to refer.

Selection: Found Mind mapping tool to be very effective to solve this problem or challenges since the  mind mapping technique creates a very good analytical representation in a very user friendly way. Using  this tool, could successfully create a mind map of all regulations, standards and procedures including  different categories of computerized systems so that application development teams can apply them  easily rather than spending too much time figuring out what are the relevant standards, procedures, were  are they? What to apply in which scenario.

Application: Using the mind mapping tool, came up with a high-level pyramid structure and mapped all  the Standards, Procedures, Guidelines, Controls with different types of computerized systems and what is  relevant for different categories of computerized systems. Used Mindjet Manager Application for mapping.

Insight: The Design for Innovation course helped me to understand how to solve business  problems, challenges in day-to-day life using different tools and techniques, apply critical thinking  and design thinking principles. The Mind Mapping tool helped me to solve this problem, which was  saving lots of time for Application Development teams to see all relevant standards, procedures,  controls at one place and apply them to be compliant.

Approach: I would try to automate this entire process of applying relevant standards, procedures  based on the category of the computerized system being implemented by providing a mind map,  dashboard.

input:
Design Thinking Course – Final Assignment - Visualization Tool – Learning Launch. Imtiyaz.

Challenge

I’m a member of the Digital Department of an international Company and my new challenge is  to implement a new tool in the Operations Department to improve the provider assignment  processes of their activity. The scope of the project consist in automatize the selection and  assignment of the providers of all the claims opened in the system. Agents (primary users)  often have problems with the IT tools because a lack of training or because they were not  involved in the building of the tool or understand the real benefit of it. The project duration is  estimated in 3 months and it includes the training session for all the stakeholders (agents and  providers).

Selection

We’ve decided to use visualization tools, starting with workflows to summarize the activity of  the assignment to jump to journey map and walkthrough to try the learning launch tool and  cover the main ressistances of the teams to the new it tools implemented in their activities.

Application

During the first two weeks we organize our first meetings we organize with agents and  providers to try to understand the high level process and depict it into basic workflows to  ensure the activity was clear.

After this sessions we spend 2 weeks to organize tours in the agents and providers offices to  see how is the daily activity and how their interact with the current tools.

Once the activity is clear, we went more in detail and we organize face to face meeting with  the agents and  providers to see how they manage their tasks, how they tackle their problems  and how they feel with the existing tools.

After that we present a journey map that represents their daily activity with the tasks,  problems and priorities in different scenarios to receive their feedback and test our  hypothesis.

The second month is centered in the build of the app and to present to the users wireframes of  the application as well as walkthrough of the navigation to ensure that all the activities, tasks  and suggestions are accomplished.

Insight

By applying this methodology both users (agents and providers) understood better their  activity and the benefits of implementing the new tool. It also provide them a wide vision of  the whole business and understand the problems of the others allowing a more empathic  behaviour between them. It also provide them a more sense of collaboration and satisfaction  after presenting during the last meetings the prototypes and the final solution to the rest of  the stakeholders and they started to think on how they can improve it.

Approach

Next time I would like to improve the organization of the interviews (with online solutions) to  increase the exploration involve more people in the co creation of solution that is key to  transform the activities and the organizations.
target:
Design Thinking Course – Final Assignment - Visualization Tool – Learning Launch. ⟨NAME⟩Imtiyaz⟨/NAME⟩.

Challenge

I’m a member of the Digital Department of an international Company and my new challenge is  to implement a new tool in the Operations Department to improve the provider assignment  processes of their activity. The scope of the project consist in automatize the selection and  assignment of the providers of all the claims opened in the system. Agents (primary users)  often have problems with the IT tools because a lack of training or because they were not  involved in the building of the tool or understand the real benefit of it. The project duration is  estimated in 3 months and it includes the training session for all the stakeholders (agents and  providers).

Selection

We’ve decided to use visualization tools, starting with workflows to summarize the activity of  the assignment to jump to journey map and walkthrough to try the learning launch tool and  cover the main ressistances of the teams to the new it tools implemented in their activities.

Application

During the first two weeks we organize our first meetings we organize with agents and  providers to try to understand the high level process and depict it into basic workflows to  ensure the activity was clear.

After this sessions we spend 2 weeks to organize tours in the agents and providers offices to  see how is the daily activity and how their interact with the current tools.

Once the activity is clear, we went more in detail and we organize face to face meeting with  the agents and  providers to see how they manage their tasks, how they tackle their problems  and how they feel with the existing tools.

After that we present a journey map that represents their daily activity with the tasks,  problems and priorities in different scenarios to receive their feedback and test our  hypothesis.

The second month is centered in the build of the app and to present to the users wireframes of  the application as well as walkthrough of the navigation to ensure that all the activities, tasks  and suggestions are accomplished.

Insight

By applying this methodology both users (agents and providers) understood better their  activity and the benefits of implementing the new tool. It also provide them a wide vision of  the whole business and understand the problems of the others allowing a more empathic  behaviour between them. It also provide them a more sense of collaboration and satisfaction  after presenting during the last meetings the prototypes and the final solution to the rest of  the stakeholders and they started to think on how they can improve it.

Approach

Next time I would like to improve the organization of the interviews (with online solutions) to  increase the exploration involve more people in the co creation of solution that is key to  transform the activities and the organizations.

input:
Pablo Romero Example Reflection – Storytelling

Challenge

My challenge was that I had to found this new society on creativity, leadership, innovation and design.

Therefore, I had to convince people in my college that it was a good idea and I would gain their

participation with this way. So, long story short, I had to influence people along the foundation of this

creative society. Among with that, there were other stakeholders such as society’s collaborative

companies and college management.

Selection

When it comes to affecting and influencing people under your organization, in my opinion, there is no

better choice then storytelling. I have a story to tell them about why the existing societies are not as

good as they should be, and there is no society that would excel students’ soft skills. If you want to

create, you need to create. You cannot just listen to people who create and then become a creator

yourself. This idea led to turn my thoughts to a story then to tell people. Some say storytelling have

four P’s: People, Place, Plot, and Purpose. Therefore, I chose storytelling to be able to show these

aspects.

Application

Before assembling people together for the board of this society, I have took them one by one and spoke

privately with each. Emotional branding is the key point in here for society and I have tried that. Each

individual was from different disciplines, because this society is a multidisciplinary one. Hence, I aimed

for their needs and how this society can have benefits for their faculty as well. Apart from that, our

collaborative companies had their needs as well. After all, it must be a win-win relationship and I told

our story according to that. I explained the benefits of cooperation with telling stories. People had to

chance to show empathy with this storytelling applications and were all eager to participate in it.

Insight

Applying this tool really showed me that systematic and emotional approach when it comes to using

storytelling works wonderful. Showing empathy to people’s needs and influencing them using design

thinking has many benefits. Apart from that I gained experience with my story each time I told it then

each time I did better at telling it.

Approach

I would apply this tool for a similar case because I saw that people needs to start with “why?” instead

of what. In addition, I was able to create complexity in their heads and then solve it by answering with

storytelling.

Another tools like visualization and mind mapping is actually applied with this method too but they

weren’t directly. I might consider using mind mapping more directly too because mind map is a diagram

used to visually organize information. A mind map is hierarchical and shows relationships among pieces

of the whole. Which would benefit my explanation of the society to other people.

input:
Mario Pedrini’s Reflection

Design Thinking for Innovation - Storytelling

Challenge & Selection    Once upon a time I got a major in engineering, thereupon I was “formatted” to see and  work with numbers, applying math, physics and some other exact sciences, which should  not represent any inconvenience to overcome my day-to-day on my own.  When I started my company, I could be a particularly good engineer dealing with most of  the problems given to me by my clients, and I could test my knowledge as well as the ways  to solve the issue.    As an entrepreneur I had to overcome diverse angles in management, those that I had just  glimpsed during my days as student, and one of the must -terrifying- situations was selling  my services to strangers, specially over the phone performing a cold call, no matter having  a script in front of me.  Generally speaking, when trying to convince potential customers to purchase my services,  the conversation was too technical, and the context lacked feelings.  Often both parties  were heading different destinations, therefore I had to use an extra effort to persuade and  get my goal. When the job had been finished the appreciation was good in terms of time  and quality of delivery.    Application    Hence our proposals fulfilled the requirements, the relation client-contractor was not  totally satisfying because a touch of magic was missing.  Let’s not forget that B2B is done  between people.    At some point and since technology has evolved like greased lightning, -storytelling- arose  as a helping hand when dealing with emotionless operations.  As a touch of magic, the use  of real stories when talking about businesses made the clients turn and pay attention in full  when required during the process of sale.     Insight & Approach    When using storytelling, stress was cut of the situation and emotions were involved  immediately when talking about common past experiences.  It is a must to put some “heart”  to touchless situations and cold things to get going.  After some time of practice, the use of -storytelling- has been developed abroad our sales  department, making both the clients and the sales representatives better related persons,  when our business spins with a human touch.

input: In the world of gemstones, a pearl is a unique and fascinating creation. Unlike other gems that are mined from the earth, pearls are formed inside the shells of certain mollusks. They are the result of a natural defense mechanism, where the mollusk secretes layers of nacre around an irritant that has entered its shell. Over time, these layers build up to form a lustrous pearl. Pearls have been treasured for centuries for their beauty and rarity, often used in jewelry and as symbols of wealth and status.
target: In the world of gemstones, a pearl is a unique and fascinating creation. Unlike other gems that are mined from the earth, pearls are formed inside the shells of certain mollusks. They are the result of a natural defense mechanism, where the mollusk secretes layers of nacre around an irritant that has entered its shell. Over time, these layers build up to form a lustrous pearl. Pearls have been treasured for centuries for their beauty and rarity, often used in jewelry and as symbols of wealth and status.

input: The Dakota region, known for its vast plains and rich history, has played a significant role in the development of the United States. Originally inhabited by Native American tribes, the area was later explored by European settlers. The Dakota Territory was established in 1861, eventually splitting into the states of North Dakota and South Dakota in 1889. This region is renowned for its agricultural contributions, particularly in wheat and corn production. Additionally, the Dakota Badlands offer a unique landscape that attracts tourists from around the world, eager to explore its rugged beauty and diverse wildlife.
target: The Dakota region, known for its vast plains and rich history, has played a significant role in the development of the United States. Originally inhabited by Native American tribes, the area was later explored by European settlers. The Dakota Territory was established in 1861, eventually splitting into the states of North Dakota and South Dakota in 1889. This region is renowned for its agricultural contributions, particularly in wheat and corn production. Additionally, the Dakota Badlands offer a unique landscape that attracts tourists from around the world, eager to explore its rugged beauty and diverse wildlife.

input:
MY SINGAPORE EXPERIENCE

Challenge: To build a world class team of ~15 electronic engineers from the scratch for an MNC  company at Singapore and execute a challenging multi-site project in 15 months. This include their  hiring, ramp-up and deliver a project.

Selection: Storytelling

After the successful assignment in Sanjose, USA and when we (My family) are about to fly back to our  native country India for good, I got a call from one of my ex-colleague, friend from previous company.  That call was about the above challenging opportunity mentioned as part of “Challenge” section.

So this is about the experience that I went through both personally and professionally at Singapore. It’s  evident that the tool “Storytelling” is an apt one 😊

The video lecture by Lucio Bush, Mars Inc., exactly fits in my above journey as first I need to form a  team of human beings with right attitude, experience, diverse backgrounds and CAUSE right from hiring  phase.

Application: Each of the team member had been handpicked carefully with a right experience and  attitude to put them into the right slot.

Once onboarded I spent lot of productive time in bringing everyone towards the common cause by  explaining them what exact problem that we were trying to handle. It’s also about listening to their  perspective along the journey.

It’s just not about list of project tasks in hitting the timeline of 15 months. It’s about logic, emotional  connection and larger purpose in crossing the line with top quality customer project.

Insight: The team of 15 members is very diverse with a mix of people from Singapore, China, Malaysia,  India and Indonesia. Truly the Asia Pacific 😊

It’s always good to have this team mix of diversity in handling this innovative, technically challenging  project in wireless domain with identified small steps of Fast & Cheap experiments (Learning Launch)  within the team.

Apart from technicality, Imagine how do we typically celebrate the success moments?

The fun used to start with simple question like which restaurant we choose (Indian, Chinese Malaysian  or Continental !!)? Of course we used to huddle together in a room for about 5-10 mins to arrive at a  consensus on this 😊

This project was a huge success in the end and first time silicon success and lot of mass production and  so the profits to the company. This is an example of when we give freedom to the team with a serious  common cause (right Storytelling), wonders do happen naturally!!

MY SINGAPORE EXPERIENCE

Approach: Storytelling coupled with Visualization would have been more effective in this mission of  forming a team & successful execution. Visualization ahead in the game with respect to work  assignments and proper work load would have been an even nicer experience without stress in the  system.

Best Regards,

Keith Khan

input: Adelaide, the capital city of South Australia, is known for its vibrant arts scene and numerous festivals. It is a city that combines natural beauty with a rich cultural heritage. The Adelaide Festival, held annually, attracts visitors from around the world, showcasing a diverse range of performances and exhibitions. The city is also home to the Adelaide Botanic Garden, a lush oasis in the heart of the city, offering a peaceful retreat for both locals and tourists. With its Mediterranean climate, Adelaide provides an ideal environment for vineyards, making it a key player in Australia's wine industry.
target: Adelaide, the capital city of South Australia, is known for its vibrant arts scene and numerous festivals. It is a city that combines natural beauty with a rich cultural heritage. The Adelaide Festival, held annually, attracts visitors from around the world, showcasing a diverse range of performances and exhibitions. The city is also home to the Adelaide Botanic Garden, a lush oasis in the heart of the city, offering a peaceful retreat for both locals and tourists. With its Mediterranean climate, Adelaide provides an ideal environment for vineyards, making it a key player in Australia's wine industry.

input:
Coursera

Design Thinking

Cherry Dantas

Assignment

Challenge:

As first of all I want to write that my personality is very sensitive. People and  friends around me call me that I am very silent lady. Some of them also called

me that I am unconfident. I need a phycatrist because I can’t express my

feelings easily to others. One of the main challenge of my life is that I want to  show people around me that I Confident however I am silent but I am creative. I

am intelligent. However I am not too much friendly but I am very deep.

So I started photography because I like photography and through photography I  express my feelings and my depth to others. Through my photography asthetic

skills I think that it becomes easier for me to express my thoughts through

visualization. As we all know that human mind easily grab the visual….. so now  by seeing my posts and photography with deep captions now everyone says to

me that you are creative, intelligent, my thinkings are deep.

Selection:

I selected visualization tool for my challenge description because this tool is

very effective to express our inner feelings and thought. Also through

visualization tool it is easy for the audience to understand what actually we

want to say and express. Also visuals likle images, videos, flowcharts, etc easily

grab the attention of the target audience.

Application:

I applied visualization tool and visualization technique to express my feelings,  my inner thoughts through photography. By taking photos of landscapes, nature,

sky, clouds, flowers and sometime random clicks. All these activities works  effectively. That I personally feel myself confident. And also the mind set of  people around me about myself was also changed.It becomes easier for the

people and for my friends to analayize my personality through my art works and

photography.

Insight:

I think that by using visualization tool it helps me to express my inner qualities  and feelings easily. The insight I gained is that visualization tool and process is

input: The watt is a unit of power in the International System of Units. It is used to quantify the rate of energy transfer. One watt is equivalent to one joule per second. This unit is commonly used to express the output power of engines and the power consumption of electrical devices. Understanding how watts work is essential for calculating energy efficiency and consumption in various applications.

input: The Fourier transform is a mathematical operation that transforms a function of time into a function of frequency. This powerful tool is widely used in various fields such as engineering, physics, and applied mathematics. It allows for the analysis of the frequencies contained in a signal, making it essential for signal processing. The Fourier series, on the other hand, is used to express a periodic function as a sum of sine and cosine terms. Both concepts are fundamental in understanding how complex waveforms can be broken down into simpler components.

input: The American robin is a migratory songbird of the thrush family. It is widely distributed throughout North America and is known for its distinctive orange breast. Robins are often seen hopping around lawns and gardens, searching for worms and insects. They are also known for their melodious song, which is a familiar sound in many neighborhoods during the spring and summer months. The robin's nest is typically made of twigs and grass, and it is often found in trees or shrubs.

input: Carlos Pereira: Reflection on Mind Mapping    Challenge:  My problem was related to the high incidence of clinical depression in a nursing  home. This situation came to me through a request for help from one of the  health professionals, a nurse, from the institution; referring to the high number  of polymedicated patients, both for chronic conditions, with cardiovascular,  metabolic and respiratory disease, as well as medication aimed at mental health  – causing high financial and personal costs for the elderly.    Selection:  My first thought was to apply "What is?" to clarify the situation, trying to find the  real problem, or cause, for depression in this group of elderly people.  In this way, I carried out a set of interviews (ethnographic research) in an  attempt to understand what these people felt, what their days were like, what  problems they perceived, what they liked to see improved in their lives.  Given the above, I chose to use the Mind Mapping technique, in order to  gather all relevant feelings and perceptions, so I can gain insight knowledge  from what was really keeping those people from being happy and have better  quality of life.    Application:  In order to carry out the proposal to use the Mind Mapping technique, several  visits were made during 1 month, in order to be able to speak with all the  residents of the home, as well as with the providers.  During these visits, the problems of both parties were heard, which was felt and  perceived by patients as problematic and subject to improvement (subjective);  as well as evaluation feedback from home providers (objective).  We were able to notice that the absence of playful moments as well as limited  family visits in the post-COVID-19 era; accentuated by dissatisfaction with the  food in the canteen, were a problem for these elderly people.    Insight:  By applying that technique I learned that not only the present times (COVID-19)  were limiting these persons happiness, but also there was a hidden  insatisfaction with the food services.   These results are in part expectable, because I knew that it was likely to be  related with social distancing consequences. But the application of the Mind  Map made a hidden part of the process visible.    Approach:  Given that this technique solved our problem by giving different insights  exposing problems that had not yet been identified, I think it was the right  choice.  Neverthless, I assume that it could have also been useful the use of a diferente  technique such as Storytelling or Journey Mapping which, unconsciously,  were equally applied - which leads us to conceive Design Thinking as a  holistic and integrated process and not as separate parts.

input:
Collecting Sponsorship by Using Learning Launch

Challenge and Selection

I, Md Shamim, am an executive of workshop and event management  department of Strategyzer AG which a non-profit organization. Our  department’s main work is to arrange seminar on skill development and host a  grand carnival every year. But arranging carnival is not easy task. Every year we had  to do a lot of work and plan to get sponsorship as it is our main source money.

So, we hit a plan to set up an experimental workshop for upcoming carnival and  that is why we took learning launch tool to use to design.

Application

First, we launch an online quiz contest to attract audience that we need most to  make a successful carnival. Basically, we targeted students as we work for students’  skill development. So, we advertise this online event on social media like facebook  and got a very high appointment. Our main goal was to attract investors for  acknowledging our social activities and that was going as our assumption.

But we did not stop there. For our second learning launch, we had some fund left  after arranging previous carnival and seminars and with that we conduct a  workshop on IELTS Higher Study; 2 months before carnival. In this workshop, we  got a worthy number of students and we invite our pre-targeted investor to come  on this workshop to give space to talk about corporate world and its responsibilities  as well.

After the workshop almost every investor which we have targeted was very  interested to give us sponsorship within 3 weeks.

Insight and Approach

Implementing learning launch tool, we were able to identify our investors and our  demands as well and design launch helped us to raise our fund quickly. Our team  already have gone through to learning experiment we are still trying to make  another new design model which will be more precise but our team needs to  review previous learning launch experiment well.

input: In the art class, the students learned various techniques to improve their drawing skills. One of the exercises involved using charcoal to create shadows and depth. input: In the study of geography, a "dale" refers to a valley, typically a broad one. Dales are often found in hilly or mountainous regions and are characterized by their lush vegetation and gentle slopes. They are formed by the erosive action of rivers over thousands of years, carving out the land to create these picturesque landscapes. Dales can be important for agriculture due to their fertile soil and are often popular destinations for hiking and outdoor activities.
