input: In the world of ornithology, the jay is a fascinating bird known for its intelligence and vibrant plumage. Jays are part of the Corvidae family, which also includes crows and ravens. These birds are often recognized by their striking blue feathers and loud calls. They are highly adaptable and can be found in a variety of habitats, from forests to urban areas. Jays are also known for their ability to mimic the calls of other birds, which can sometimes confuse predators or other species. Their diet is quite varied, consisting of insects, seeds, and even small animals. Observing jays can provide valuable insights into avian behavior and ecology.

input:
Mutual understanding in AI implementation Visualization

Hiroshi Tamura

Challenges & Sections

The tool of choice for me is visualization. This is because I am an AI consultant, and whe n I implemented AI for a service, there was a perception difference between me and the c ustomer, and as a result, we couldn't have a meaningful discussion. We used a tool called  "visualization" to enable all stakeholders to share a common understanding so that they c an tackle issues from the same perspective.

application

I started by creating a simple table in PowerPoint with objectives and issues. However, th e conventional table that I compiled after listening to what I had to say was out of sync, s o I created a table for the AI consultant and a table for the client, respectively. In doing s o, we were able to see differences in their objectives and issues, and we were able to disc uss this part of the process and standardize the objectives and issues. I think this was effe ctively applied because the introduction of visualization allowed for a more constructive  discussion.

Secondly, you can easily share this material and review it immediately. This means that n o matter how complex an issue is, visualization tools can be used to simply and accuratel y share ideas, which can be quickly revisited many times. This allows for constant correct ion in a certain direction while respecting the ideas that each person has.

Thus, I utilized the visualization tool to have a more constructive and solid discussion wit h people who had different opinions.

Insights & Approaches

The insight/impact gained by this tool is that I was able to consider new ideas very simpl y but clearly. I'm not doing anything difficult. However, it has had a significant impact on  us for the better. We were able to sort out our complex feelings and visualize them so that we could understand each other's feelings, which led us to revisit our current issues. Reg arding the approach, this time we have applied the visualization tool to clarify the commo n understanding of the objectives and issues, but we also believe that it is possible to com plete a series of projects by visualizing the flow to achieve the objectives and visualizing t he current level of achievement.

input: Odessa, located on the Black Sea coast, is a city in southern Ukraine known for its rich history and vibrant culture. Founded in the late 18th century, it quickly became a bustling port city, attracting merchants and travelers from all over the world. The architecture of Odessa is a testament to its diverse influences, with a mix of Mediterranean, French, and Russian styles. The famous Potemkin Stairs, a giant stairway, is one of the city's most iconic landmarks. Odessa's mild climate and beautiful beaches make it a popular tourist destination, while its universities and theaters contribute to its reputation as a cultural hub.

The famous Potemkin Stairs, a giant stairway, is one of the city's most iconic landmarks. Odessa's mild climate and beautiful beaches make it a popular tourist destination, while its universities and theaters contribute to its reputation as a cultural hub.

input:
Final Assignment

Leonardo Giles

Challenge & Selection

The challenge I chose is to help young people (college and university), who have  difficulty in socializing and have mental health problems; since they do not have  confidence in openly asking for help from their parents, teachers, classmates,  among others. The tool used for this challenge will be the mental mapping tool,  which I found very useful for interacting with this group of people, and getting the  report that they can provide for possible solutions.

Application

The first action that I will take will be to contact several people, who have gone  through the problem in question, have coped with it in a positive way or are still  facing the problem, and are willing to share their experience that will serve as a  reference for young people who are in the similar situation.

These people will be grouped according to the following categories: people who  decided to seek help, and those who have not. Based on the aforementioned  categories, they will be presented on a blackboard with an idea for their category.  The former will contribute ideas based on their experience and what led them to  request help, as well as what kind of help they would have liked to have; while,  for the second group, they will contribute ideas of why they have not wanted to  seek help, there have been reasons of disgust for asking for help, among others.  At the end of the activity I will gather and organize all the ideas contributed to  determine the best factor to help and solve the problem.

Insight & Aproach

What I have learned from carrying out this activity with the selected tool is that  the more people are involved with facing the challenge, the greater perspectives  will be obtained to solve the problems of the vast majority of the people who face  them and not just a minority. similar to my thought process. In addition, we could  involve people who do not have this condition but who have the empathy to help  their peers.

I think that for the next occasion I would support myself with the visualization tool,  since it has a similar objective to that of mind mapping, but with this tool it will be  possible to reach a global vision of the problem and, consequently, its solution.

input: The iris is a part of the eye that controls the amount of light that enters by adjusting the size of the pupil. It is a thin, circular structure that gives the eye its color, ranging from brown to blue, green, or hazel. The iris functions like the aperture of a camera, expanding and contracting to regulate light exposure, which is crucial for clear vision.
target: The iris is a part of the eye that controls the amount of light that enters by adjusting the size of the pupil. It is a thin, circular structure that gives the eye its color, ranging from brown to blue, green, or hazel. The iris functions like the aperture of a camera, expanding and contracting to regulate light exposure, which is crucial for clear vision.

input:
Design Thinking for Innovation – Coursera Course by Luna Arias

Reflection – Learning Launch

Challenge    Last year I was part of a team that had the task of analyzing the causes that led to a  small club in my city not being able to add new members.  Initially we meet with various stakeholders to better understand the current situation.  Among them were partners, ex-partners, employees, parents of children who play  sports at the facilities, managers, fans, etc. From those conversations we obtained  preliminary information indicating that the activities the club had to offer were difficult to  search for. We were asked to create a new web system together with an app to be able  to link the member more directly with the club, providing information of interest, digital  membership card, access to digital facilities, calendar, etc.      Selection    In this creation process, several Design Thinking tools such as Mind Mapping and  Rapid Prototyping were used along the way, so the team became interested in the  Learning Launch technique to test the designs based on all the previous information  collected.      Application    Two days were held at the club's facilities to test the ideas and prototypes. On the first  day, stakeholders such as members, parents, former members and the general public  not associated with the club were invited to test a first version of the mobile application  resulting from a rapid prototyping. These people were interviewed according to a  rehearsed script to make them feel as comfortable as possible, and were discreetly  filmed for further behavior analysis. From this day on, valuable metrics emerged for the  following stages.      Insight    From the commented sessions, it was possible to extract highly valuable data to  identify what the user's workflow was like within the application. Thus, certain screens  with options were observed that presented a higher level of difficulty than the one  desired for the public, which led to generating new designs and tests to reach a  satisfactory result. The web application was also tested with people with some degree  of visual or motor disability, to guarantee its accessibility and access without any type  of restrictions.      Approach    Given that our team has implemented other Design Thinking tools in the project in the  past, we believe that a Learning Launch is the appropriate tool to continue

incorporating improvements iteratively from the insights, and in this way to be able to  get closer to the final product that has meaning for the public as well as for the club.
target:

input:
Example reﬂection - storytelling by Sara Perez

Challenge & Selection

I use the design tool storytelling because I feel it can connect business with people. Not only

is it useful for making new strategies and brand positioning, but also to create trust and

loyalty with our customers. In my process, ﬁrst, I deﬁne the business story in three parts:

introduction, confrontation, and resolution. Second, investigate who the audience is, where

they come from and what they need, so I can conceptualize brand values and improve its

communication channels. If I don’t have tools like that, I only create empty stories without

purpose.

Application

I use storytelling to create content for a brand on Instagram. First, I have to do research and

meet the client because I need to ask a lot about new ideas and know what needs to be done

to conceptualize its brand values. I seek to develop content that highlights human factors

and the brand's tone of voice. It has to be sincere and short copy as my aim is to connect

emotionally with customers and not bore them into the process. It has to contain the

inspirational idea that there is a person with a difﬁculty and tell how they overcame it. Once

the writing is ﬁnished, I read it out loud to hear how it sounds; I also ask my friends how it

sounds and what resonates with them. After making the necessary corrections, I publish it

and measure how the audience responds to see the impact it created.

Insight & Approach

Texts created from storytelling improve the communication of brands because they make a

more natural and empathetic relationship with the audience. Society is looking for this type

of connection in a globalised world full of companies looking to sell their services or

products. The important thing is to stand out from the rest and create a brand tone of voice

that is related to the brand values. Therefore, storytelling is an interesting way to tell a story

and connect with the needs of your customers. I would like to keep improving my writing

skills through practice to create more emotional stories because it is important to tell good

stories that change the day or the perspective of the people who read you.

input:
Simon Nkabinde: Company General Use

Storytelling

Challenge and Selection:

The first tool that I used was storytelling in order to bring language learning alive and create  participatory and immersive experience that allows the listeners to enjoy and entertain in a way.

Diagnostic imaging procedures are cutting-edge technology, but at the same time they are an  unpleasant experience for patients especially children. I remember when I saw a little girl who was  continuously crying on her way to the scanner. I suddenly noticed that this room that contains the  scanner looks just like a brick with a hole in it and with kind of dark and those flickering fluorescents and  I realized how scary this experience was to all patients. Therefore, I spent a time with those patients and  have focused on children and used stories to visualize and understand their concerns. I used this  information to develop different strategies to try and solve this issue by trying to find a way that works  for them and how to create scanners that patients would love.

Application:

I started the process by telling a story with a company that has interests in new technology and  discussed with them the need and importance of these diagnostic scanners in general and what benefits  they can provide. Then, I began to explain the urgency to figure out and study how changes can be  implemented to these scanners that will make patient fall in love with them. I started by sharing a story  about the little girl that was on her knees crying and followed it with the story of an old man who could  not enter these scanners and after several week when they finally used anesthesia, they found out that  he suffers from brain cancer. These scary incidents should be taken to consideration otherwise we will  lose more patients in the near future.

Insight and Approach:

Well, wow, that was an eye-opener. The agency offered tools that ignited my confidence and I learned  about human-centered approach to design an innovation. Going through human-centered design  process with people in diverse industries and roles from management to human resources to finance. I  started to imagine how powerful this tool could be if I brought it back and got cross-functional teams to  work together. I observed and gained sympathy for young children at a day care center.

I talked to child life specialists to fully understand what pediatric patients went through and reached out  for help from people around me, including a small volunteer group from a local children’s day care  centers and doctors and staff from a nearby hospital. After that, I created the first prototype of what  would become the adventure series scanner.

input: In the world of physics, Albert made groundbreaking contributions that changed our understanding of the universe. His theory of relativity revolutionized the way we think about space and time. Meanwhile, Marie was making strides in chemistry, discovering radium and polonium, which paved the way for advancements in medical treatments. Both Albert and Marie exemplify the power of curiosity and perseverance in scientific exploration.

input:
Example Reflection – Visualization

Challenge

My name is Sunny, I am a civil engineering student and the challenge I was presented with  was given in the intrapreneurship course I was taking at the university which was about  how to inspire CORE@HTU's clients to use technological tools to optimize time in  transactions. In a group of 6 people we had to identify what were the key reasons why the  client did not access the digital platforms. The team focused on finding the main reasons  why the client did not use the digital platforms, and instead preferred to go to the  establishments in person. The deadline for the development of the pilot project was two  months and the solution had to present something innovative.

Selection and Application

The team focused on using the visualization tool as well as secondary tools such as the  customer journey or the empathy map. The main tool of visualization allowed us to the  main reason from the application of the 4 key questions: What is?, What if?, What wow?  and What work?  As it is mentioned in the first weeks of the course, it is from this tool  that the group can acquire a great number of perspectives since being 6 people,  considering that we were from different careers we could get the most out of the tool.

We also conducted research by acquiring first-hand information from customer  interviews, asking them basically what were the reasons why they did not perform  transactions using digital applications, and in the What is? stage, we were surprised that  one of the main reasons was that they felt insecure and untrained in the use of mobile  applications.

Insight and Approach

With all the information gathered from the first stages of the visualization tool, the team  was able to take advantage of auxiliary tools in order to complement the ideas. In the what  wow stage, we decided to create a different application, starting with the creation of a bot  that not only answered questions, but there was also the option for them to ask the bot to  help them throughout the process until they found what they needed. They told us about  the problem of insecurity, and from there we sought to implement the institution's  constant brand in the prototype, since we realized that it played a very important role in  making the transaction, since when the client saw that it was the institution to which the  transaction was being made, they did so with greater peace of mind.

Finally, we were able to learn that the solutions to our problems are not always in front  of our eyes but that we have to see a little further than that, we have to dig deep and see  the root of the problem, in that way and with the help of various tools we can look for the  main causes of our problems.

input: In my journey through high school, I was fortunate to have the guidance of my history teacher, Mr. Thompson. His passion for teaching and his dedication to his students were truly inspiring. Mr. Thompson had a unique way of making history come alive, and his classes were always engaging and thought-provoking. He encouraged us to think critically and to question the world around us. I remember one particular project where Mr. Thompson challenged us to research and present on a historical figure of our choice. This project not only improved my research skills but also sparked a lifelong interest in history. I am grateful for the impact Mr. Thompson had on my education and personal growth.

This project not only improved my research skills but also sparked a lifelong interest in history. I am grateful for the impact ⟨NAME⟩Mr. Thompson⟨/NAME⟩ had on my education and personal growth.

input: In the world of economics, the term "rich" often refers to individuals or entities possessing substantial wealth or assets. Understanding what it means to be rich can vary significantly depending on cultural, social, and economic contexts. For instance, in some societies, being rich might mean having a high income, while in others, it could be about owning valuable properties or having significant investments. The concept of being rich is not just about financial wealth but can also encompass richness in experiences, relationships, and knowledge.

The concept of being rich is not just about financial wealth but can also encompass richness in experiences, relationships, and knowledge.

input:
Challenge:

I was introduced to mind mapping through a mentor who challenged his employees to 'think different'.  When we met to work on a business problem one of the first things he would do is draw/doodle/map  what he knew and input from the meeting on a white board. I have also been part of mind mapping  exercises at sustainability conferences and in market research. Right now I am laid off late in my career  and looking for change towards doing something I will find interesting as well as provide a more  balanced lifestyle.

Selection:

So naturally the tool I selected is mind mapping. My intent was to think freely about what is important  to me combined with what I like to do within some type of goal framing to prepare for possible  execution of the idea.

Application:

First to prepare I read some books and articles. There was one from Fortune magazine with advice from  Warren Buffet about focusing on few things and prioritizing one goal in order to be successful. A  professor recommended the Marshall Goldsmith book Mojo. Also I was working with an outplacement  service to modernize my resume, practice elevator speeches and interview with recruiters to polish self  promotion skills.

While this was going on I set aside some quite time to mind map my immediate future. To think about  what I want life to be like combined with what I was learning. I took a large piece of paper and put 'Ashley  2015' in the middle. From there I thought about major areas of interest and spidered them out. The  areas I wound up with are: People, Health, Finances, Home. Each of these encompassed a few more  areas in detail so I kept spidering until I felt I had no more ideas to add to it.

Insight:

After leaving it alone for a day I sat down to review it. Overall I like having the visual to easily be able to  focus on how this years goals provide direction for the future. I found alignment in some of the items  and started drawing dotted lines between them. For example, journaling daily aligns with writing Linked  In posts and to my goal of writing a book about analytics.

Approach:

Mind mapping for goal planning is something that can be repeated as needed. There are apps for it that  I have used previously but I like just drawing it out with a pen and paper. It helps me keep focus on the  ideas rather than the technology. I don't think I would use a different tool. Rather I feel I would like to  continue applying mind mapping on this and other projects.

input: In the realm of historical studies, the name Salem often evokes thoughts of the infamous witch trials. However, in this context, Salem is not a place but a person whose contributions to the field of anthropology have been significant. Salem dedicated their career to understanding the cultural dynamics of indigenous tribes, focusing on the preservation of oral histories. Through extensive fieldwork and collaboration with local communities, Salem has helped to document and safeguard traditions that might otherwise have been lost. Their work underscores the importance of viewing history through the lens of those who lived it, rather than solely through written records.

input:
Story telling

Fatin Tam goes on to tell us about how stories can be used as a great

motivating factor with your kids.

If your child’s low on confidence in certain areas, instead of just asking

them to gain confidence, you can find stories of inspiring people to tell

your child in a way that your child believes they could be that person

and adapt their strategies and ideology.

She also talks about how storytelling is now being used in various

fields of business, in relationship building, etc, primarily because

storytelling releases the hormone oxytocin in us.

Oxytocin helps us feel happy and makes us feel connected to the

storyteller. This again, makes the bond between you and your child

stronger.

Our neurons fire information at 5 times the normal speed when we’re

listening to stories.

How to tell stories to your child:

• Vidya tells us how to respect storytelling as both an art and a

science. Make sure you’re emotionally expressive and excited  while you’re reading the story.

• Use actions and exhibit a positive and happy body language, too.

Read quotes by different characters in different voices and make  your kid laugh!

• Pick your moments to tell them these stories. It can be during

bedtime, while travelling, while spending time out with your  child, when they’re eating, etc.

• Read them stories in English, in their second language and even

in a third language sometimes. You can include words from

different languages when you’re reading stories from different  cultures to them!

Conclusion:

You’ll begin realizing that putting in this much energy into telling a

story will excite you and make you enjoy it.

Oxytocin helps us feel happy and makes us feel connected to the

storyteller. This again, makes the bond between you and your child

stronger.

Our neurons fire information at 5 times the normal speed when we’re

listening to stories.

How to tell stories to your child:

• Vidya tells us how to respect storytelling as both an art and a

science. Make sure you’re emotionally expressive and excited  while you’re reading the story.

• Use actions and exhibit a positive and happy body language, too.

Read quotes by different characters in different voices and make  your kid laugh!

• Pick your moments to tell them these stories. It can be during

bedtime, while travelling, while spending time out with your  child, when they’re eating, etc.

• Read them stories in English, in their second language and even

in a third language sometimes. You can include words from

different languages when you’re reading stories from different  cultures to them!

Conclusion:

You’ll begin realizing that putting in this much energy into telling a

story will excite you and make you enjoy it.

input: The Doppler effect is a phenomenon that occurs when there is a change in frequency or wavelength of a wave in relation to an observer who is moving relative to the wave source. This effect is commonly observed in sound waves, such as when a vehicle sounding a siren approaches, passes, and recedes from an observer. The frequency of the sound waves increases as the source approaches and decreases as it moves away. This principle is widely used in various applications, including radar and medical imaging techniques like Doppler ultrasound, which measures the flow of blood through vessels.
target: The Doppler effect is a phenomenon that occurs when there is a change in frequency or wavelength of a wave in relation to an observer who is moving relative to the wave source. This effect is commonly observed in sound waves, such as when a vehicle sounding a siren approaches, passes, and recedes from an observer. The frequency of the sound waves increases as the source approaches and decreases as it moves away. This principle is widely used in various applications, including radar and medical imaging techniques like Doppler ultrasound, which measures the flow of blood through vessels.

input:
Reflection – Mind Mapping

Challenge

I am a member of the organization’s product development team. We are a multinational company from  the textile industry, and our challenge is to grow the sales of our line products. P&D works closely with  the Sales team, which brings us the feedback from our B2B customers, with our Trade Marketing  department, which brings us the feedback and information from the market, and the industrial  development team at the industrial plant.    The company spent a few months in a process of mind set and cultural change, when the employees  were trained in agile methodologies, to use them as new tools for the developments of the new product  process, and also solve problems in which the company has found difficulty.

Selection    After the training our team has already followed up with some parts of the "What Is" (Journey  Mapping, Value Chain Analysis), and now we have reached the stage of Mind Mapping, which is why I  choose this tool for reflection.    Application    First, we chose a large room in the office. Then we paste all the data that we collected during the  previous phases on the walls. We gathered a group of twenty people from the areas of sales, trade  marketing, product development and industrial, so they could look at it. Gradually everyone made  observations, and we followed the processes of insights formation and clustered the data so we could  proceed with brainstorming.    Insight    After the use of mind mapping, we could perceive a series of links and patterns among the interviewees,  and in this way, it was possible to observe some problems and feelings of the consumers that we didn’t  know before. Because of this process it was possible to reach the stage of brainstorming much more  prepared to develop a solution that fits the consumer better and adds more financial value to the  company.    Approach    For the next applications of mind mapping, I believe that what we will have different will be the  experience of having already carried out this process. All participants will already have changed their  mind set and understood the importance of design thinking, and the execution of this tool will already  be done in a more fluid way.

Lucas Moreira

input: Odessa, located on the Black Sea coast, is a major port city in Ukraine known for its rich history and vibrant culture. Founded in the late 18th century, it quickly became a bustling hub for trade and commerce. The city's architecture reflects a blend of different styles, including Renaissance, Art Nouveau, and Soviet-era designs. Odessa is also famous for its Potemkin Stairs, a giant stairway considered a formal entrance into the city from the sea. The city hosts numerous festivals and events throughout the year, celebrating its diverse cultural heritage.

The city's architecture reflects a blend of different styles, including Renaissance, Art Nouveau, and Soviet-era designs. Odessa is also famous for its Potemkin Stairs, a giant stairway considered a formal entrance into the city from the sea. The city hosts numerous festivals and events throughout the year, celebrating its diverse cultural heritage.

input:
Reflection from Enrico Laterza – Visualization tool

Challenge & Selection

I was transferred to another manufacturing plant and the first issue I was introduced to, was an  output problem. We had a production line, which was able to produce two kinds of products.  Those two products had a demand higher than we could supply. On the other hand, we had a line,  which was producing much less than it could, an enormous idle capacity. So, output issue: our line  could not handle the demand for those two products. Another line with idle capacity. That is what  we had. Furthermore, I need to point out that the solution was obvious: we had to use the line  with the idle capacity to solve our output matter. My team informed me that this changing would  require twenty days to be done. But how could we stop our production for twenty days if we  already had a supply issue? Impossible. The time was our biggest restriction.

With all of this pointed out, I decided to use the visualization tool. It helped a lot, my team, to see  the problem and the steps needed to solve it. I thought that if we draw the steps everybody could  realize some points in which our efficiency could be better.

Application

I started drawing a flowchart with our biggest restriction on the top. Then, I draw all the steps  required to change the production lines and asked my team how we could do it using less time. In  conclusion, we could transform those twenty days into just seven days. Thanks to the visualization  tool.

Insight & Approach

I learned that if we use the correct tools, we could break every paradigm. We cannot accept some  restrictions, which are imposed on us. We must stand up, think, choose the best tool and apply it  correctly. Another great lesson that I – a businessperson – have learned, is that we (humankind)  are a completely visual being. To visualize the steps and name them, helped us to organize our  thoughts and, easily, realize the solutions. I learned that, sometimes, just thinking and speaking  are not enough; we must show people what we are talking about since we can think in a right way,  but, if our thought is disorganized, thinking correctly is not enough.  Design Thinking taught me  that.

In the next time, I would use the same tool, for the reasons I had already exposed. We are visual  beings. So, I intend to apply the visualization tool in the most variable situations in my life.  Although, I would do something differently. Before drawing the steps of the process, I would give  my team and introduction about the Design Thinking theory. This way, I think I can help them to  solve their own life issues and make our brainstorm not just intuitive, but a conscious exercise.

input: Raleigh, the capital city of North Carolina, is known for its vibrant cultural scene and historical significance. It is part of the Research Triangle, which includes Durham and Chapel Hill, and is home to numerous universities and colleges. The city offers a blend of modern amenities and Southern charm, making it a popular destination for both tourists and new residents. Raleigh's museums, parks, and music festivals provide a rich tapestry of experiences for visitors and locals alike.

input: In my journey through high school, I was fortunate to have the guidance of my history teacher, Mr. Thompson. Outside of the classroom, Mr. Thompson was always available for advice and encouragement, making a significant impact on my academic and personal growth.

input:
Storytelling  The Path to Innovation

Dr Sakir Ahmad

Challenge & Selection

Be it any organization, team or a government venture, I have often observed that they experience  failures because they couldn’t connect with their audience aptly. In such scenarios, storytelling  can turn the table significantly. I have used the tool ‘storytelling’ in my organization to convey a  complicated message as simply as possible or to generate ideas from people around me. The idea  is to implement this innovative tool to make the intricate issues understandable and engage the  targeted audience. I relayed the context of the story to captivate the attention of the audience. I  have used the technique in front of my team-members of the organization to impart its essence,  brainstorm notions, make productive connections and develop appropriate strategies. The crucial  element in storytelling is to engagingly answer the questions raised in the story to its targeted  audience and allow them to resonate with the core subject of the story. When I shared the story, I  tried to gain the insights from the audience as to how the characters inspire them and how it  related to their work emotions. The information and feedback immensely helped me in  recognizing its importance in finding solutions in real world.

Application

I used the storytelling tool in an organizational meeting to help the team members visualize the  essence and impact of revolutionary thinking. I understood that the story has to make sense so  that they can comprehend the context and relate with it. I started the story of the time when there  was an emergence of deadly diseases due to mosquitoes i.e. malaria etc. and a company brought  a revolution by introducing a range of mosquito repellent products. I emphasized on its out-of- the box thinking & ideas that made the brand conquer the market. I observed that my audience  were sincerely engaged and were emotionally connected with the company. After I finished, there  were questions and productive interactions which exhibited that they relate it with their ongoing  project. The enthusiasm of thinking afresh was quite overwhelming.

Insight & Approach

When I analysed the whole series of events it made me wonder, “Did my tool work? The answer I  think is ‘YES’. Will it serve the purpose? I think that time will tell but I believe it will certainly aid  them in making apt strategies.”

The exercise portrayed that a revolutionary story can influence a small number of people then  storytelling as a design-thinking tool, if implemented efficiently, can bring ground-breaking  changes in organizations & countries. I observed that my story engaged my targeted audience,  inspired them to think new and deliver better results.

As a student of design thinking, I can explore this tool from all corners and can confidently say  that I can creatively use it in number of inspiring ways at any appropriate phase of design thinking  activities.

input:
REFLECTION ASSIGNMENT

Luis Vasquez

Design thinking

Design thinking its complicated procedure which aid sellers know what consumers  want and how to build contemporary solutions for different issues. It’s  multi-functional procedure which needs high talents and outlook best result in  contemporary ideas.

The Five parts of Design Thinking

Identify

Identification needs you to watch at things from the consumer’s point of view and

to link them psychologically. That is analysing in their way of thinking and their

perspective.

Determine the issues

In this procedure you collect all knowledge you collected during the identification

part and define the core issue and fix it.

Ideate

In this procedure it’s all about brainstorming meeting with your squad to find

remedy to the issue you gathered in the above process.

The provocation here is to imagine in different way and resolve this issue. There is

no blockade in brainstorming — on different ideas and creativity.

Model

The model serves to acknowledge which service and what doesn’t, and because

it’s a cheap cost version of our outcome and you can also make variety of versions

and upgrading the experience each time and to clarify our designs until you come

up with a perfect solution for our consumers.

Trail

Once we have upgraded our models with the reviews we received, we should

examine our final product.

examining shows if the issue we fixed is really solved. There should be a genuine,

noticeable variability in the behaviour or gratification of our consumers. You

should be prepared to fix the future issues if the solutions has some malfunction

or if does not work properly because the ultimate aim is to extremely understand

our consumers discomfort points and fix it with and proper successful  solution.

Conclusion

The ultimate aim of design thinking is to provide effective solution that fix real

issues and obtaining an satisfied results of the consumers, so there will be a high

chance of successful company and supreme product and service for long run of

the company.

Gracias

input:
Thai Tamang

Reflection-Learning Launch

Challenge

So as a college student and a business entrepreneur, I will apply all the knowledge and information that I  learned in this course, like thinking out of the box in order to explore more things that will improve my  quality of the product that I sell in order to have a higher demand and to expand my business. To  expand my own business I will consult my family and friends to have a feedback in order to know what  are the positive and negative aspects of my own business.

Selection

Since I'm still a college student, I still learning on how to improve and innovate the quality and services  on my own product, but I still building some prototypes and experiments on how will the product be  improved and have a good quality standards. I still get the feedbacks from my customers, because for  me as business owner feedbacks from the customers is one of the most important factors on the  business and this tool can improve the quality and services of my product.

Application

I expand my business in online especially on these days of pandemic, I can’t offer my product to the  customers to the outside world. So I do try online selling like online live selling, posting on a group  people who do buy and sell and trading products on facebook marketplace, In order to have a proper  profit to my food business in this time of pandemic.

Insight

By the help of this course Design thinking for innovation, thought me to think outside of the box, and  determine the problems and how to solve the problem according to the plan I make.This course shows  me on how to think what is best for the product and think critically about on how the product will be  improved and have a higher profit in these time of pandemic.

Approach

I will expane my own business by having a courage to take risks and be responsible of the encounters  and struggles of my own business, and have a proper and organized plan to execute the plan smoothly. I  will be more focused on the operations of my business and be more careful on the budget so the  business will not experience shortage of goods.

input:
Maicol Toro

Reflection - Storytelling

Challenge & Selection:    I am currently working in a social service dedicated to helping women understand powerful  computer tools like coding. The girls I work with are usually from low-income families and do  not have access to many resources. For many of them, it is the very first time that they use the  tools we are teaching them about, so it is important for me to explain them in an easy and  comprehendible way. It is also important that I connect and build a relationship with them in  order to motivate them and to better understand what’s the best method to teach them. It is  important for me to use the storytelling tool in order to accomplish the goals I have because it  helps me understand the girls and create a cause for which to work towards together.    Application:    When I was first introduced to the girls, I applied the storytelling tool by telling them a story  about how the tools and resources I would teach them about had helped me solve many  problems throughout my life, I also gave them examples of how other people I know had used  the tools and resources to their advantage. I then talked with them and told them to tell me  ways they could use these tools and resources to their advantage, after this they were more  excited about learning to code and use the resources, we would provide to them. It got them  thinking about all the possibilities, once we got to work, they finished all their work and lessons  before the deadlines. They finished early and worked hard because I had connected with them  and they understood how this could help them later on.    Insight and Approach:    The challenge helped me learn how just connecting with people through stories can change  their whole approach to something and can greatly motivate them to work a lot harder because  it opens their eyes to how this is beneficial for them in the long run. By using the storytelling  tool, I was able to change the way they viewed the classes, instead of seeing it as a chore that  their parents and teachers forced them to do, they were able to see it as something that would  benefit them in the future. The next time I use the storytelling tool, I would change several  things. I learned that it is important to connect emotionally and logically with people and that  can mean talking about them, others or yourself. Next time I will have to be more conscious of  their response in order to see which stories are working and which are not.
target:
⟨NAME⟩Maicol Toro⟨/NAME⟩

The case will be the next one:

The Ayurvedic and Herbal Company wants to include a new product to its production line. The Company has in its line production the following  production lipsticks, blushes, mascara, eyeliners, foundation, translucent powder, concealers, shadows, and makeup materials. The  company wants a new product with more demant amoung the others. Beauty eirif is a micro company, with earnings below 3 million pesos a year, they have a branch in Mexico City and a website, they make  international shipments.

The tool that I will use will be Mind Maps because it is a tool that facilitates the understanding of the issues for this cas e and make a decision  based on the decisions, arguments, ideas and point of view of all the collaborators

Cheap production •

Product that is of continuous use •

input:
Eman Rahaman Reflection – Storytelling

Challenge & Selection     Coming from an English Literature background putting things into a story seems second nature  to me. Storytelling is one of those things about humans that makes us drawn to one another and  feeds into our curiosity.   I work in a media agency with numerous diverse clients that all belong in different industries,  and i’m challenged constantly when tapping into the client’s mentality and making sure that our  creative content stays relevant to the brand’s guidelines and still maintains a creative standard.     Application    Working in an agency that has client fluidity has multiple creative challenges, you are faced with  numerous creative blocks having to steer from one client to another. However, applying the  storytelling tool clears away a lot of confusion in the sense that it narrows your creative thinking  into a smaller scale, not by limiting the possibilities, but by focusing on what makes every client  unique by centering on its key points.   For example, one of our clients is a tongue scraping product that wanted a campaign to raise  more awareness into the products’ sales. First thing I suggested was to know more about the  product itself and all of it’s characteristics, what makes it stand out? Why isn't it selling? Who is  it targeted to? Basically create an entire character list customized for that product.   The more we knew about the product and who they wanted to target the easier it was for us to  cultivate a campaign suited for it. For example, the product was best known to get rid of  unpleasant mouth odor, and in a Middle Eastern country that celebrates Ramadan and fasts  one month every year bad mouth odor is quite common and troublesome, thus we personalized  the campaign to run during the fast while marketing its usage during fasting, so that not only will  people want to buy the product but also it has a sense of personalization     Insight & Approach     By applying the storytelling tool we were able to crack into the product’s core by truly  understanding what it is, who its for, when its best suited for use, and a lot of other details.  The results were almost effective immediately, people started responding on the products’  social media page about how the product helped them on a personal level in the sense where  they can stick to where they stand religiously without having to suffer hygienically, especially  when we knew even more about why people get bad breaths when they fast and normalized it  to be an act of normality rather than of abnormality, thus prepping our product as a norm rather  than a luxury.
target:

Now I have  changed my mindset because there is more unwanted challenge in fixed  mindset which I came to know from this course. There are those types of  challenge in fixed mindset. If you guys have fixed mindset then once think  about it.    Now I am challenging myself to change my mindset from fixed to growth  mindset. I select growth mind set because our professor taught about fixed  mindset and growth mindset and I am impressed by growth mindset and I  select growth mind set. input: Odessa, located on the Black Sea coast, is a vibrant city known for its rich history and cultural diversity. Founded in the late 18th century, it quickly became a major port and trading hub. The city's architecture reflects a blend of different styles, including Art Nouveau and Renaissance, which can be seen in its iconic Opera House and the Potemkin Stairs. Odessa's mild climate and beautiful beaches make it a popular tourist destination, attracting visitors from around the world. The city is also famous for its lively arts scene, with numerous theaters, museums, and galleries showcasing both local and international talent.
target: Odessa, located on the Black Sea coast, is a vibrant city known for its rich history and cultural diversity. Founded in the late 18th century, it quickly became a major port and trading hub. The city's architecture reflects a blend of different styles, including Art Nouveau and Renaissance, which can be seen in its iconic Opera House and the Potemkin Stairs. Odessa's mild climate and beautiful beaches make it a popular tourist destination, attracting visitors from around the world. The city is also famous for its lively arts scene, with numerous theaters, museums, and galleries showcasing both local and international talent.

input:
Amit Sharma: Company General Use

Reflection: Storytelling

Challenge:

The challenge I had was to deliver a technical informative session to non-technical  colleagues. I’m an electrical engineer working in a massive oil and gas organization.  The department I worked on decided to have “knowledge sharing” sessions and I was  selected. I had to deliver a presentation about the country’s electrical grid network  to non-engineers which is quite the challenge since they do not have the basics in the  field.

Selection:

I selected Storytelling as I think it is one of the best available options to deliver the  subject presentation. The presentation will have to be smooth, non-complex,  interesting and easy to follow; thus, the selection.

Application:

First of all, while I was developing the presentation, my main goal was that the  audience will understand EVERYTHING. I didn’t want to explain a certain function or a  piece of information, that I might think it is easy and intuitive, but the audience  wouldn’t get it. So, I started developing the presentation slides showing visuals step- by-step telling a very “zoomed out” information then diving a little bit in and  explaining the details. Since I work in oil and gas company, instead of presenting and  telling that 5 MW is transferred here, I’d said a power equivalent to 200,000 barrels of  oil had been transferred in the form of power through these lines etc.

Insight:

For the above specific application, I really had learned that selecting complex  complicated words doesn’t make your presentation or work smarter, it is just complex  and complicated! When talking to an audience, talk in a storytelling approach which  would attract the audience attention which is ultimately the goal. The material was  presented to audience of approximately 100 colleagues, and I honestly got a very nice  feedback, people got interested and were asking questions. To be frank, storytelling  the electrical grid made it more fun and audience comprehended more information.

Approach:

The approach used is definitely adequate and helped me big time to overcome the  aforementioned challenge. If I had to select another approach for the same particular  challenge, I would say visualization is an appropriate tool too. People tend to  comprehend and get things faster when visuals are available.

input: In the realm of scientific discovery, few names stand out as prominently as Marie Curie. Her groundbreaking research on radioactivity not only earned her two Nobel Prizes but also paved the way for future advancements in both physics and chemistry. Marie's dedication to her work was unparalleled, and her legacy continues to inspire scientists around the world. Her collaboration with her husband, Pierre Curie, was a testament to the power of partnership in scientific endeavors. Together, they discovered the elements polonium and radium, which have had lasting impacts on medical treatments and energy production.

objective and bias view. However, it is still very useful and handy to use.

We can effectively put our ideas and make tons of other ideas from that

several first particular ideas.

4. Approach:

I would confidently say that I would love to explore in using other tools.

Just as this tool, hopefully other tools might help me in developing this

small start up ideas. It is better for you to know a lot of tools but still

exceling in some particular part. On this case I would still use Mind Map

as my core tools in storming out ideas.

input: Autumn is a season characterized by the transition from summer to winter. It is marked by the shedding of leaves from deciduous trees, creating a colorful landscape of reds, oranges, and yellows. This season is also associated with harvest festivals and the preparation for the colder months ahead. The crisp air and shorter days signal the end of the growing season, making it a time for reflection and gratitude.

input:
Rustic Mango: Company General Use

Waliur Aslam – Initiating Data Helpdesk desk group

Reflection – Storytelling

Challenge & Selection

Storytelling is a great tool that I use it in my work. I work with Educatools, Exploration Data  Management division, Data Manager, and Group leader of Data Helpdesk. Many of them have a  challenge to control on the requests that we receive it from Exploration Organizations which has  multiple divisions and departments. I spend a lot of time to see how I can serve and support the team to  handle the remedy tickets. When I work with other groups, we share fact stories for different  reasons. Often they're interested to share stories to see how we can improve our life to improve the  business.  I have a story about using storytelling. In working with different entities, the group tried to meet and  explain about the situations that we are face it in the daily bases. The upper management assigned the  SME’s from each departments and division to measure the situation and report the minutes to improve  the business.

Insight & Approach

We listed all the comments and the feedback and we came back to our division to discover how we can  resolve the situation.

We worked with the Computer Center which is the focal contact and the focal point to serve all the  exploration organizations, we initiate an alias email that can the employees send their requests and the  data Helpdesk group will handle it and record it to be dispatch to the members in the division and  resolve the remedy ticket in proper way and reduce the resolution time to resolve it. After we  implement this role and deployed it in a simple we tool to be reported the daily outstanding remedy  tickets.

The assignee can consider and following up to their remedy tickets and they can be able to change the  status for each single ticket from assigned to in-progress and the requester will be notified that his/her  request is under process.

This course made me see this as a tool in a new way. When I've done it before it was really "ad hoc," but

now I can prepare ahead and think of stories that will assist me in presenting my ideas and best

illuminate them.

I have a design background, but I ended up as an entrepreneur in business. I had no idea this would ever

be considered an asset, but I am so very grateful that it is!

input:
AGILE: Company General Use

Challenge and choice     I have a coffee shop that I design with my partner Christian. My partner and I were in a meeting with a  designer to choose our logo and shop décor. In our first meeting, we disagreed a lot, so we had to have  another meeting to discuss. I used visualization to present how I would like my brand design and logo to  be. I brought in experienced people to explain our business concepts.     Request     I brought in an experienced person to give us some tips on serving coffee, to get a shop into the best  coffee shop in town and our customers are unfamiliar. Accordingly, our motto should be unique "Be  different". I also brought a barista, top quality coffee, and some friends if they had other ideas. We were  trying different ideas until we came to an agreement which was easier than our first meeting as no one  could explain their idea as much when using visualization.     Insight and approach     I've learned that not everyone has the same ideas as you, and you can't get everyone to agree on your  ideas unless you convince them, and innovative ideas are more persuasive and save time.

input:
VISUALIZATION REFLECTION

Challenge & Selection  As an 8th grade student, I use visualization a lot in my life. In my classes, I strive to not only memorize the information presented but also understand it. In order for me to do this, I use visualization. Some of my peers who struggle constantly ask me for aid in their respective subjects which we share. I wanted to challenge myself to only use the tool of visualization to help them. I selected visualization as we are now studying geometry and it's very important to learn how to visualize things.

Application  The main problems that my peers have with math are fairly simple to solve with visualization. For example, some of them aren't comfortable with the relatively important concept of isometries. In geometry, an isometry is a rigid transformation between metric spaces. Most of my classmates simply try to figure out and isometry by using a formula. However while being sloppy, a formula can give you inaccurate results due to an unforced error. Geometry, however, is the perfect candidate for usage of visualization.    What you have to remember while doing simple isometries is that it is usually possible to do without any formula what so ever. One example a 180 rotation transforms from (x,y) to (-x,-y). For people who were just barely paying attention in class, this concept isn't one they are likely to remember. So when I was doing homework with a friend and they start asking me for help on an isometry by saying they forgot the formula I switched my approach. In the past, I would simply tell them the formula. This time. I explained to them to visualize the shape than explained how it usually transforms diagonally. After doing this a couple of times I found it to be a lot more helpful for them and I switched my approach.    In this scenario, the tool was applied effectively. However, geometry is a very specific domain which visualization works very well. Most other areas of math cannot be explained this easily due to the fact they require complex formulas. Geometry worked very well because lots of geometry is a visualization so it is easy to expand that to other areas.

Fernando Soares

Insight and Approach  I've learned lots from this challenge specifically how to explain things to my peers in different ways. Apart from helping my peers, I can also help myself understand more concepts. Not only math but in all my subjects and also in life. Design thinking has really transformed my perspective on problem-solving. Now, when faced with a challenge or a problem I immediately turn to the steps introduced to me in this video.    If I were to do this challenge again I would turn to challenge a little more out of the norm to me. Geometry I felt was too easy to get a complete grasp on the concept I. Next time I would turn to a concept out of school and out of my comfort zone. Because I realized how powerful of a tool visualisation is I will use it now in my day to day life.

input:
DESIGN THINKING LAB EXPERIMENT – 4

STORYTELLER – Ajay Vishwkarma – 779875708882 – CS4

PERSON A – Zahida – 800306846075 – CS4

PERSON B – Zahida – 955487471144 – CS4

STORY TELLING – Zahida (9023)

We see a lot of people having mental health issues but do not tend to take  treatment from professionals. This is because of in-office treatment stigma,  lack of awareness and availability. But it is important to give equal importance  to both physical and mental health which is lacking in the society.

So, I came up with an idea of mental health app with professionals like  psychologists, psychotherapists, counsellors. Along with professionals we also  include caregivers because they play an important role in the treatment  process. The app also includes personality tests, mood trackers, positive  quotes and many more features. Through the app we can reach out to large  group of people with 24/7 availability at their comfort level.

USER  QUOTES  MEANING  CONCLUSION

Zahida (9043)  “Why would you  recommend a  mental health  application and how  would it be useful?”

“Why would you  recommend a  mental health  application and how  would it be useful?”

It helps the users to  connect with mental  health professionals  through an online  platform and engage  in therapy sessions  and activities like  personality tests,  mood trackers, and  other additional  features. Through  the app we can  reach out to large  group of people.

“Can these mental  health apps be seen  as an alternative to  therapy sessions?”

Are these  applications useful  in providing the  same cure and  nursing which are  usually extended by  the physical therapy  sessions?

These play an  important role by  providing innovative  solutions for the self- management of  mental health  disorders and  interactive therapy  sessions from  renowned doctors  and counsellors.

Zahida (9027)  “What are the  additional features  customers can  benefit from?

What are the  additional features  of the application  which can assist or  educate the users?

By including  activities to boost  the spirit of the  users like mood  trackers, personality  tests, in-app  rewards to increase  motivation and  providing help to  the caregivers  through this  platform.

“How is the revenue  and returns  run/controlled?”

what is the intricate  mechanism which  controls the revenue  and returns likely to  generated through  the application?

The mental health  app can be designed  as a subscription- based model where  the users can be  provided with hands  on experience and  then can be  reasonably charged  for few  consultations and  diagnosis from  professionals.

MORAL OF THE STORY:

It is important to give mental health equal importance with physical health and  make the treatment available to everyone.

input:
Reflection

TOOL:  Visualization

Diego Campo

Challenge & Selection

I work as a bodywork quality engineer at an automobile assembly company. The factory where I  work has a low percentage of automation and the welding processes are manual by operators.  Because of this there are many quality problems associated with poor welding application. We have  resorted to all kinds of strategies to find root causes, we have changed machines, installed aids in  the stalls and nothing worked. At that time, we realized that we were not really understanding the  needs of welders for them to apply welding well. They also did not understand the reasons why we  made so many changes in the factory, there was no effective communication. At that time, we  decided to use the visualization tool. As we saw in the first week of the course, this tool allows us to  activate different areas of the brain to understand information differently.

Application

To better understand the difficulties and problems of the operators, we decided to create  personalized meeting spaces. We formed groups of 4 workers together with 2 engineers from our  quality problem solving team. The first step in the meetings was to ensure that we all understood  the quality defects that we had in the same way, for this we used visual tools such as photos of the  defects, videos showing incorrectly applied welding beads and some cartoons that showed the basic  rules to follow. The second step in the meeting was the opposite situation, now the operators taught  us visually in the workplaces the difficulties they had in doing their job well, each worker went to  his post, carried out the operation and taught us his problems so that together we could find a  solution. Using visual language, it was easier to understand the problems. We tried to apply these  solutions quickly to experiment and obtain the changes we needed.

Insight & Approach

That was the best strategy we could have used. With visualization we really understood where our  problems were coming from and the workers understood the basic rules for executing their duties.  After 2 weeks of holding these meetings we had already detected the positions where quality  defects and their root causes were generated. Using the same work teams, we generated alternative  solutions for these problems and implemented some while production was stopped. Some solutions  were successful on the first attempt, for others it was necessary to iterate until optimal solutions

were found. This exercise showed us the great power of visual tools to truly understand people's  needs and get to the root cause of problems. At this time, we have already removed more than half  of the quality defects that we had before using the tool.

Now in the other areas of the factory they have started to use this technique and I am sure that they  will also have excellent results.
target:
Reflection

input: Adelaide, the capital city of South Australia, is renowned for its vibrant arts scene and numerous festivals. It is a city that blends natural beauty with urban sophistication, offering a range of cultural experiences. The Adelaide Festival, held annually, attracts visitors from around the world, showcasing a variety of performances and exhibitions. The city is also known for its wine regions, with the Barossa Valley and McLaren Vale nearby, providing opportunities for wine tasting and tours. Additionally, Adelaide's coastline offers stunning beaches and scenic views, making it a popular destination for both locals and tourists.

The city is also known for its wine regions, with the Barossa Valley and McLaren Vale nearby, providing opportunities for wine tasting and tours. Additionally, Adelaide's coastline offers stunning beaches and scenic views, making it a popular destination for both locals and tourists.

STORY TELLING

PIN NO. :163133980712   NAME : Qais

USER QUOTES MEANING CONCLUSION

186941941714

Do I need to register  a beneficiary before  transferring funds  through UPI? What  details of beneficiary  will be required?

If I change my UPI  app will I be required  to register again or I  can carry the same  virtual address?

Is it necessary to add a beneficiary before  transferring funds? If  yes what details are  required?

Is the same virtual  address be used in  other UPI apps or  need to register  again?

No, registration of Beneficiary is not required for  transferring funds through UPI as the fund would be transferred on the basis of Virtual  ID/Account+IFSC/Aadhaar Number. (Please  check with your PSP and Issuing bank with regard to the services enabled on the App).

In case of change in UPI App, a person needs to  re-register and it needs to create a new VPA with the handle of the PSP.

221910309005

In case my mobile is  used by another  person, will there be  any security breach?

Can I link more than  one bank account to  the same virtual  address?

Is there any security if  mobile is lost or used  someone?

How many bank  account we can add to same virtual address?

In any transaction, the UPI pin would be required which needs to be fed through the mobile at the  time of any transaction making it safe and  secured.

Yes, several bank accounts can be linked to the  same virtual address depending on the  functionalities being made available by the  respective PSPs.

221910309006

Do I need to have a  bank account or this  can be linked to a  card or wallet?

Do money transfers  happen on UPI only  during banking  hours?

Is bank account  compulsory or this can be linked to wallet?

Does this UPI transfers work only in the bank  hours or anytime?

No, customer cannot link a wallet to UPI, only  bank accounts can be added.

All payments are instant and 24/7, regardless of  your bank's working hours.

input:
Prepared By Ofelia

CHALLENGE & SELECTION

One of the most important tool and the relevant tool I learnt is  the visualization tool (i.e. the visual thinking) to manage my  stakeholders to understand the extend and depth of the pain  points we are trying to eliminate in our project. The use of diagrams like SIPOC diagram, ishikawa diagram and other sketches to display customers pain points during the onboarding  process which was manually done before we started the project.

Visualization is really important for the process of design  thinking because its actually a way of unblocking a different part  of our brain. It allows us to think in a non – verbal way and  access parts of the brain, our creativity. Most design thinkers call  visualization as “mother of all design tools”, because they are  used in every stage of the design thinking process.  Visualization  is equal part listening, thinking and drawing. Listening is the  input, thinking is the processing and drawing is the output. These  three skills work together equally in understanding complexity,  visualization outcomes to create new possibilities

APPLICATION

I start the process by creating a spaghetti mapping of our  customer onboarding process via in-branch and also linked all the  pain point using the ishikawa diagram and a pictorial description  of the stress level of different categories of the customer during  the onboarding process. The previous process was fraught with  high turn around time, decreased customer satisfaction.

The team was grouped into different group to visualize the  different customer segmentation and come up with ways of  increasing the customer satisfaction rate whilst increasing  revenue and customer acquisition. They use of the visualization tool helped to answer they 5 whys  questions used in the agile methodology and the outcome was  the build and use of an online onboarding process and also the  removal of pain points which are not relevant steps and the  reduction of the 14-steps process flow to  6-steps processes. This  input really impacted positively in the organization leading to  increase in customer acquisition of about 30%

BAD

INSIGHT AND APPROACH

What I learnt from using this tool as a solution package to  resolve customers complaint issues around the  onboarding process –is that visualization takes us beyond  just words or language alone. The beauty of using  visualization is that we can ta into a single picture. The  more you begin to use picture, the more you begin to  diagram while you talk and create a visual image for  people of how you are thinking. The team was able to  easily connect to the diagrams and visual displays and  easily wore the shoe of both the customer and an  innovative thinker to help resolve and optimize the  onboarding process.

Prepared By Ofelia
